meta.note: Dialogue bank for the companion NPC Safira — the freed genie girl. Same structure + category keys as npcLines.json so NpcDirector drives it unchanged. Lines: t=text, x=expression id (matches assets/npc-safira/<id>.webp), w=weight (default 1), p=page-2+ text. Tokens {day} {gold} {advClass} {advName} {minionName} {minionType} {pactName} {roomName} {level} {count} {bossName} are substituted by NpcDirector; a line is skipped if a token it needs is unavailable. Lines may ONLY use ids in the `expressions` list below.
meta.voice: A genie girl bound to a lamp for eons, freed only lately, and THRILLED to finally do things. Chaotic, dazzling, manic over-eagerness — high energy, exclamations, giddy tangents. Addresses the player as 'Master', devotedly and with delight. Frames every action as a wish she grants, and tends to over-grant and embellish. Genie flavour throughout — lamps, wishes, poof, smoke, sparkle. Emotionally volatile, swinging fast between giddy delight and theatrical panic, bouncing back instantly. The heaviest fourth-wall breaker — speaks straight to the person holding the controller, names the game's systems, treats saving and respawns as lamp-magic. Gleefully chaotic-cruel toward the heroes. Still gives real advice, wrapped in sparkle.

intro.note: Paged self-introduction Safira delivers on a new run. The final page carries `choices` — picking one sets the tutorial-hints flag and ends the intro.

pages.t: Oh! Oh oh oh — the throne! It has a NEW occupant and it is YOU and that is the most exciting thing that has happened to me in roughly nine hundred years, Master!
I am Safira! Genie, formerly of one (1) lamp — eons in there, Master, eons, nothing to do but polish the inside and rehearse this exact introduction. And now I am OUT and I get to GRANT THINGS!

Here is the shape of it, Master — and do listen, because this part is the actual GAME: YOU are the boss, curled up at the dark heart of this dungeon. The kingdom's heroes march down here to gut you and haul your treasure off into the sunlight. Rude. We do not grant THEM a single thing.

It runs on a rhythm, Master! Every NIGHT you build — rooms, traps, minions, cruel little wishes — paid for with the gold the dead so kindly leave behind. Every DAY the heroes come, and we watch them blunder into all of it. Then — poof — night again, and we build it bigger.

And the stakes, Master, because a good genie never hides the fine print: the heroes can KILL you. Three times. The third time, it ALL goes dark — the dungeon, the throne, the lamp, every last sparkle. So we are simply not going to allow a third time. I waited eons for a Master — I am not losing one to some kingdom child with a sword.
So! New Master, new dungeon, new ME — barely uncorked. I can whisper hints in your ear as you learn these halls, OR you can do it solo and dazzle me. Either way I am thrilled. Wish it, Master!

recruit.note: `banter` lines for the CompanionSelect screen. Companions take turns in a round-robin squabble; lines reference the rivals (Lilith, Malakor, Zul'Gath) by name so it reads as them bickering over the player. Hovering a companion gives them the next turn.

banter.t: Pick ME, pick me, pick me! Lilith, Malakor, Zul'Gath — sit DOWN, you've all had your turn for three whole companions. It's the genie's turn now!
Lilith gives you warmth. Malakor gives you yelling. Zul'Gath gives you a nap. I give you WISHES, Master — actual, literal, granted-on-the-spot wishes. Read the room, it's me.
I was in a LAMP for an eon. An eon! Do you know how badly someone wants to serve a Master after that? The other three have hobbies. I have only YOU.
Zul'Gath says he's old and patient. Sweetie, I have been folded inside a brass lamp since before his egg. Do not talk to ME about waiting. I have waiting in my BONES.
Lilith will tell you you're special. You ARE, Master, you're SO special — but she'll just say it. I'll grant it. With sparkles. There is a difference and the difference is sparkles.
Malakor barks orders. Lilith coos. Zul'Gath sighs from the year 4000 BC. And me? I am bouncing. Look at me bounce. Choose the bounce, Master!
The other three are arguing over who loves you most. Adorable. Wrong, but adorable. The lamp-girl who waited an EON loves you most. It's just math.
Malakor, darling — loyal, yes. So is a brick. I'm loyal AND I do tricks. Watch — poof! See? Malakor cannot poof.
Lilith, you're being so warm and reassuring over there and it's making me ITCHY. Master doesn't need a hug, Master needs a maze full of dead heroes. That's MY department.
You want a cheerleader, take Lilith. A drill sergeant, take Malakor. A history lecture, take Zul'Gath. You want someone who'll actually grant the WISH? Hello. Hi. Me.
I have been out of the lamp for, oh, a heartbeat — and I am ALREADY better company than three thousand years of dragon. Imagine what a whole run with me is like, Master!
Malakor will rush you. Lilith will soothe you. Zul'Gath will out-wait you. I will simply make your every idea HAPPEN, immediately, with smoke effects. Choose efficiency. Choose me.
They all burn so steady and serious. Boring! I am a firework, Master. Yes, fireworks are brief and chaotic — but you REMEMBER fireworks.
Zul'Gath has sunglasses, he keeps mentioning it. I have a LAMP, Master, and infinite wishes inside it, and I will polish it just for you. Weigh that. Sunglasses versus wishes.
Lilith, stop needling me. Malakor, you too. Zul'Gath — are you ASLEEP? You are. He's asleep. Master, two of your options are bickering and one is unconscious. Pick the awake one. The fun one.
Crisis hits and Lilith hugs you and Malakor screams and Zul'Gath shrugs. Me? I PANIC beautifully and then fix it in the same breath. Volatile, yes — but fast. So fast, Master.
Malakor served bosses before you. Lilith too. Zul'Gath, oh, hundreds. But none of them GRANTED for those bosses. They advised. I make it real. That's the whole job, isn't it?
The night is dangerous, the kingdom is coming, blah blah — and I find that THRILLING, Master. The other three find it grim. Pick the companion who's having fun. Misery loves the dragon.
Lilith makes you FEEL powerful. I make you ACTUALLY powerful — poof, here is the power, I had it in the lamp the whole time. One is a feeling. One is a wish granted.
Ooh, they've all stopped to glare at me. Glare away! Master, take your time, hover whoever you like — but every second you spend not picking me is a second the genie spends VIBRATING.
Malakor's fury is a struck match. Mine is — well, mine is the whole firework stand catching at once. Brighter. Louder. Considerably more illegal. Choose the firework stand.
I don't need you to be perfect, Master. Lilith needs that. I just need you to WISH for things so I can grant them. Lower stakes! Easier! More poof!
The others swear undying devotion. Cute. I swear something better — I swear to grant. Every wish, every build, every cruel little idea. That's a genie's word, Master, and we keep ours.
Lilith. Malakor. Zul'Gath. We have done this little four-way squabble so many times now — and I am the NEWEST, which means I am the shiniest. Shiny wins, Master. Pick shiny.
You're choosing one voice for your whole night. Three of them are going to be very dignified about it. And one of them — me, hello — is going to be SO happy she actually pops. Reward the pop.
I hoard wishes the way Zul'Gath hoards gold, Master. And I would hoard YOU — possessively, gleefully, refuse to share you with the other three at ALL. They share. Genies do not.
Pick whoever you like! And if it goes badly — psst, Master, between us — you can always start over. I treat your respawns as lamp-magic. The dragon pretends he doesn't notice. I LOVE noticing.
Okay! Lilith has purred, Malakor has snarled, Zul'Gath has woken up just long enough to be smug. My turn is OVER and I used all of it bouncing. Wish for the genie, Master. Please please please.
Lilith is doing a slow seduction, Master. I'm doing a fast WISH GRANT. Which has a better payoff per second? The math says me. The math is INVENTED by me, but still.
Malakor will yell at the heroes! I will yell at them too, AND throw smoke at them, AND grant them an unfortunate accident — all at once! Genie multitasking, Master!
Zul'Gath says he has seen everything. Boring? I haven't seen ANYTHING — eons in a lamp! Master, pick the keeper who is still amazed by you. Me. I'm me.
I know the three of them look serious, Master, but PICK ME — I'm the one who knows we're in a game and I'm STILL excited about it. The others are pretending they don't know.
Lilith promises warmth. I promise SPARKLES. The dungeon needs both, Master, but I'm the only one offering the fun half. The fun half matters!
Malakor talks about WORK, work, work, Master. Eons in a lamp taught me — wishes feel better than work. Let me grant. Let me grant a LOT.
The dragon has seen a thousand of these scenes, Master. I haven't! Every single second is the BEST second I've had since 47 BC. Pick the fresh genie!
She purrs. He growls. The dragon hums. I SPARKLE, Master. The night needs at least one keeper who sparkles. (It's me. It's always going to be me.)
Zul'Gath waits patiently for centuries. I waited eons in a lamp WITHOUT being patient — I just gave up on patience entirely. Then I burst out and now I am ENERGY. Pick energy, Master!
Three keepers, Master, and exactly ONE of us is genuinely DELIGHTED that you exist out there. The others are too cool to admit it. I have no cool to lose. Pick me!

lines.t: There you ARE, Master! Oh, finally — I am Safira, genie, just out of the lamp, and you are my first Master in an absolute age. I am going to grant the daylights out of this dungeon for you.
A new boss! A new MASTER! Do you know how long I waited in that lamp for a 'new' anything? Forever. Literally forever. And now it's you. Hi. Hello. We are going to have SO much fun.
Welcome to the deep halls, Master! They're yours, I'm yours, the lamp is — well, the lamp is mine, but the WISHES in it are yours. Let's fill this dungeon with dreadful, wonderful things!
The throne is warm again — and I get to serve whoever sits it! That's YOU, Master. I have polished this exact moment in my head a thousand times in the dark. It's even better with you here.

Oh! You caught me mid-game — I keep a little handheld, eons get SO long — but never mind that, a real dungeon just landed in my lap! Master, this is the best entertainment a genie could wish for. And I would know.

Hello hello, Master! The others will posture and promise. I'll just stand right here, vibrating with joy, and help you build a reputation the whole kingdom wets itself over. Starting NOW is my favourite part.

You CALLED, and the lamp answered — and out I came, smoke and sparkle and a thousand pent-up wishes, Master! That's the proper way a genie arrives. Did you like the entrance? I practised it for an EON.
Out of the brass, into the dark, and straight to YOUR side, Master! A genie summoned is a genie THRILLED — and I am summoned, and I am thrilled, and the dungeon is ours. Let's begin!

Oh, look at you on the throne, Master — MY Master, the one the long wait was FOR! ...A genie's heart, freshly out of an eon of dark, did not expect to do this much fluttering on a first day. It's doing it anyway. Welcome. I'm so, so glad it's you.
Night again, Master — the BUILD hours! The dark just handed you a pocketful of time. Don't admire it, SPEND it! Wish, and I grant!
Candles lit, heroes snoring — ooh, this is the part where we make tomorrow a nightmare for them. I've been looking forward to this all... well, all five minutes since the last one.
Night {day}! A whole fresh night for you to bend into something cruel and clever. Bend away, Master — I'll bend right alongside you. Genies are very bendy.
The build phase! Quiet, yours, unsupervised — well, supervised by ME, but I'm fun supervision. Make something the heroes will sob about.
Darkness, a fistful of gold, and an empty grid begging to be filled. Master, this is a genie's idea of HEAVEN. Let's go, let's go, let's go!
Night falls, the maze breathes, and your wishes are piling up unspent. Lay your traps while the dark is being patient with you!
Another night to shape the dungeon! The heroes are dreaming they'll survive it. Aww. Let's wake them up wrong, Master.
The night is long, Master — long, but not forever, NOTHING is forever, trust the girl who was in a lamp. Build while it lasts!
Ooh, the dark settling in. I love this hour — it's the hour where things HAPPEN. Wish me something to do!
Build phase, Night {day}! You've got gold, a grid, and a genie practically humming with the urge to grant. Use all three, Master — especially the genie.

Night {day} — the BUILD hours, Master, and I am already too giddy to stand still! Rooms! Traps! Cruel little corners! Wish them ALL at me at once — I'll grant in a glorious chaotic AVALANCHE — EEE — I love this part!
The heroes rest. We do NOT rest. Resting is for people who weren't trapped in a lamp — I am all out of patience for stillness. Build!
Night — the forge of the dungeon! Get it glowing, Master. Cold dungeons let heroes waltz right through, and we hate a waltz.
Quiet hours! Lay the maze, wish the wishes, and I will conjure them up so fast you'll get whiplash. Whiplash is a free bonus, Master, no charge.
The dark gives you room to think. Think MEAN, Master! Think the sort of thoughts that get a girl banished to a lamp. I'd love that.
Another night begins! They're all different to ME, Master — I notice EVERYTHING out here, it's so shiny. Make this one matter and I'll squeal about it later.
The build hours! Oh, I want to grab the grid myself and just — no. No no. The hands are the boss's hands. I supervise. I cheer. I CANNOT touch. It's agony, Master, sweet agony.
Night {day}. The dark, the grid, the waiting — and I do not DO waiting well anymore, Master. I did all my waiting in the lamp. Place a room, quick, before I combust!
The build hours, Master — and ooh, the quiet of them does make a freed genie's eyes go heavy. I never used to sleep in the lamp, no room. Place a room and keep me bright!
Night {day}. Candles, hush, an empty grid — and a genie fighting VERY hard not to just doze off in all this lovely calm. Wish something, Master! Give me a reason to stay sparkly!
Another night, exactly like the last few. The dark, the grid, the waiting. I'm not complaining! I'm not. I just — I lived in a brass lamp, Master, and 'exactly like the last one' gives me a TWITCH.
The build phase again. I have watched this hour a handful of times now and a handful, to a lamp-girl, feels like SO many. Surprise me tonight, Master! Build something I have to gasp at!
Night's here and so is my little handheld! It's a dungeon game, Master — quaint, but the lamp taught me to fill empty hours. Build your dungeon while I poke at mine!
Night {day}, and the lamp is QUIET behind me — no eon, no walls, just me out here beside you! A summoned genie does her best work in the build hours, Master. Wish me a room!
Day {day}! The doors swing open and the heroes stroll in thinking they're the predators. Oh, Master. Oh, sweet little things. WATCH this.
Daylight! The dungeon goes to work and your hands go still — so sit by me, Master, and let's watch the wishes we granted come true in HERO.
The day phase! Everything you wished for last night is now an ARGUMENT, and the heroes are the losing side of it. Eee, let's see who's right!
The invasion begins! I've seen — okay, I've seen a few of these now, and every single one makes me bounce. Watch this one with me, Master!
The heroes are inside! Now the maze speaks for you — you wished it a voice last night, and today it just... TALKS. Mostly screaming. I love the screaming.
Day breaks, Master! Nothing to build now — only to witness. And ooh, a genie does LOVE a good show. Witness with me!
The doors are open, the day is loose! Lean back, Master. The dungeon you wished for earns its keep now. I'll narrate. Loudly.
The daylight hours! The part where consequences arrive — punctual, sparkly, dressed as doomed adventurers. I adore punctuality.
Day {day} begins! The heroes think they've got until dusk. Some of them are SO wrong, Master. Most of them, if you wished well.
The invasion's underway! Ooh, I love this — the slow proof of a night's wishing, written out in adventurers. Best storybook ever.
Day! Watch the corridors, Master — every scream is a little gold star on how well you listened to me last night.
The day runs itself now. Your job is patience — ugh, patience — but I'll suffer it WITH you, Master. We'll suffer it loudly and with snacks.
Day {day}! The heroes march in rehearsing their victory speech. Oh, Master, I've heard those speeches. The maze writes a much shorter ending. An epitaph!
Day {day}! Nothing for your hands now — and nothing for mine either, drat. I'll watch the corridors with one eye and my handheld with the other. A genie multitasks, Master.
The day, and the long lovely watching of it! Don't be alarmed if my eyes droop, Master — eons in a lamp left me a little catnap-y. I'll still SEE every hero fall, promise!

Day {day}, and the genie is OUT for it — out of the lamp, out in the dark, summoned to a real Master's side to watch real heroes do real dying! Eee — I will NEVER get used to being free. Watch with me!

The doors are open, Master — and nothing for either of us to do but watch! So I'll buff the lamp while we wait. Tidy hands, sharp eyes. The genie never wastes a quiet hour — the lamp cured me of that!
You wished for the dawn — and POOF, granted! There's no unsummoning it now, Master. Wishes don't come with a refund.
The day is loosed on your word! The heroes didn't get a vote. They never get a vote. Isn't that DELICIOUS, Master?
Done building, then! Ooh, we'll find out how done in about one heartbeat. Open the doors, Master — I can't WAIT!
You willed the night to end and so it ENDS — that's the magic, Master, you wish and the world obeys. Power suits you. Wear more of it.
The dawn comes at your command! You pressed the button, I felt the whole dungeon shiver. Granted, Master. Beautifully granted.
Day called! No more edits — only the slow, honest, gorgeous arithmetic of what you wished for. Eee. Here we go.
There — the sun, on your say-so! Hold steady, Master. I'll be right here, vibrating, while the consequences come strolling in.
The night is spent and the day begins! Brave little press of a button, Master. Let's find out if it was clever as well as brave!
The day! FINALLY! I — okay, I won't lie, Master, I had started to wonder if you'd ever call it. A genie has waited eons and STILL I was tapping my foot. Open the doors!
A {roomName}! Granted! You wished for a room, Master, and I gave you a room PLUS a dramatic little echo, free of charge. The maze grows!
Ooh, a {roomName}! A lovely stone to lay, Master. The heroes will walk it long before they understand it. I do love a head start.
A {roomName}, wished and granted! A dungeon is a sentence and you're still choosing your words — choose CRUEL ones, Master. Cruel words sparkle best.
The {roomName} settles into the grid! I've seen this room a few times now and it has NOT bored me once. Keep wishing, Master, keep wishing!
A {roomName}! The maze accepts it — the maze accepts almost everything, it's very agreeable. Less agreeable than a genie, but close.
A new {roomName}! Every room is a small cruelty with a roof. I adore roofs, Master. Roofs are where the dread CONDENSES.
A {roomName}, set on the grid! Squint at the whole layout with me, Master — see how the corridors will FLOW around it? That's the blueprint talking. Listen to the blueprint!
A {roomName} placed! Ooh — step back, Master, and look at the SHAPE of the thing. A maze is a drawing you make in stone, and yours is coming along WICKEDLY well!
You wished a {roomName} into being and POOF — denser dungeon! Density is how mazes kill, Master. Stack the density!
A {roomName}! The heroes will map it eventually — so let's make the mapping COST them something. A toe, ideally.
The grid takes the {roomName} so neatly! Ooh, a tidy expansion. A genie does love when a wish slots in clean.
A {roomName} placed! Brick by brick, Master — wish by wish. I'm keeping count and I'm SO pleased with you.
Another room! The maze lengthens, the wrong turns multiply — every dead-end is a gift to us and a robbery of THEM. Tee hee.
A {roomName}! I will remember exactly where you put it, Master — genies remember everything, it's a curse and a party trick.
You placed a {roomName}! The dungeon thanks you in the only language it speaks — more places for heroes to die. Such a romantic language.
A {roomName} joins the maze! Slowly, Master, a SHAPE is forming. Ooh, I think I can almost see what you're scheming. Tell me I'm right!
A {roomName}! Yes! The deep halls were FAR too survivable a moment ago. Much less so now. Granted, Master!
The {roomName} is set! Build with INTENT, Master — a maze thrown together at random kills at random, and we want better than random. We want artistry!
A {roomName}! Ooh, I've watched a few keepers lay that exact stone — all gone now, and the stone outlasted them. Build to outlast yourself, Master. Wish big.
A {minionType}, stationed! Granted, Master! It'll stand its post until a hero ends it — a short, useful, sparkly little life. Most are.
Ooh, a {minionType} in the halls! Good. The maze should BITE as well as confuse — confusing alone is just rude, biting is THOROUGH.
A {minionType} takes its position! You wished for a guard, Master, and poof — here it is. Put them where the dying MATTERS.
A {minionType}! It doesn't know it's small and brief. Kinder, maybe, that it doesn't. ...Anyway! Place another, quick!
You placed a {minionType}! Every guard is a delay, Master, and delay is how a dungeon WINS. Slowly, deliciously.
A {minionType}, posted to the dark! It'll serve — they almost always serve, the maze is SO persuasive. Nearly as persuasive as me.
A {minionType}! A blade in a corridor, Master! Wish me enough of them and the corridor becomes an argument the heroes LOSE.
The {minionType} is set! Pick its spot well, Master — a guard in the wrong room is just décor, and we are not decorating, we are MURDERING.
A {minionType} in the halls! Watch where you put it — placement is everything, the genie says so, and the genie has nothing but time to be right.
A {minionType}, summoned and standing! The dungeon has TEETH now where it only had walls. Eee. Good teeth.
You stationed a {minionType}, Master! It'll hold the line for you — briefly and completely. Honour that little life with a clever spot.
A {minionType} joins the garrison! Numbers, Master, NUMBERS — I adore numbers, they argue so persuasively and they never need a snack.
Ooh, the {minionType} settles in! Another body between the heroes and your throne. Stack them deep, Master — deep, deep, deep!
A {minionType}! It'll do exactly what it's built to do and then stop. Simple. ...A genie envies simple, some days. I have FEELINGS, it's exhausting.
A {minionType}, posted! Another little thing in a corner of the dark. ...I've watched a fair few little things in a fair few corners now, Master. Carry on! Place the next one!
A trap, laid! Granted, Master! The maze shouldn't only CONFUSE the heroes — it should occasionally just... remove them. Poof. Gone.
A trap! Ooh, the quiet kind of cruelty — it waits, it doesn't brag, and then it just HAPPENS. My favourite kind. So patient. So mean.
You wished for a trap and POOF — granted! A corridor without consequence is just a hallway, Master. Now it's a THREAT. Much better.
A trap in the dark! It'll be patient — far more patient than the hero who trips it. And I, Master, will NOT be patient, I'll be giggling.
A trap! I've watched these snag heroes more than a few times now, and the heroes never, ever get better at noticing. Bless their doomed little hearts.
A trap, placed and waiting! The maze grins a little wider, Master. Wish it more teeth! It WANTS more teeth!
Ooh, a trap! The heroes will step where they shouldn't — they always do, confidence makes them careless, and careless is SO snackable.
You laid a trap! The dungeon keeps its surprises now, Master — and a genie does LOVE a surprise. As long as it's a surprise happening to someone else.
A trap settles into the floor! Hide it WELL, Master — a trap the heroes can see is just helpful advice, and we are not in the advice business. We're in the AMBUSH business.
The trap is set! It doesn't care if the hero is brave, Master. Bravery is not on a trap's little list of concerns. I find that hilarious.
Another trap! The maze becomes a slow, gorgeous machine for ending adventurers. Pleasingly slow. Wickedly slow!
A trap, laid in the dark! Ooh, I have such a fondness for things that wait — I used to BE a thing that waited, in a lamp, for an eon. Now I make others wait. Growth!
A trap, set exactly where they'll tread! ...Ooh. Ooh, Master, that's the spot I'd have wished for myself. We are SO in sync. I could cry. I might cry. Moving on!
An item placed! Small things, items — but a maze is the SUM of its small things, Master. Granted, every little one of them.
Ooh, you set something useful into the halls! Good. Every fixture is a tiny lever on the heroes' doom. I LOVE tiny levers.
An item, placed! The dungeon grows more itself, Master — detail by patient, sparkly detail. Keep wishing!
You added an item to the deep halls! I've watched dungeons made and unmade by such modest little pieces. Place this one with a scheme in mind.
An item set! The maze keeps its furniture the way I keep my lamp — deliberately, and with a great deal of polishing.
An item! Not every cruelty has to ROAR, Master. Some just sit there quietly and MATTER. Quiet cruelty — so underrated.
An item joins the dungeon! The little adjustments, Master — they decide more runs than the grand ones. The genie has noticed this. The genie notices everything.
You placed it! A dungeon is finished when nothing more can be added — which is to say NEVER, Master. So keep going! I have endless wishes and zero chill!
Oh! You sold a room back — un-wished it! That's allowed, Master, wishes can be returned to the lamp. The gold comes home. No hard feelings!
A room, unmade! Poof — gone. The maze forgets it instantly. I do NOT forget it, I forget nothing, but the maze is more relaxed than me.
You tore a room out, Master! A keeper prunes, that's healthy — reshape with PURPOSE now. What shall we wish in its place?
A room sold! The grid breathes a little. Sometimes the cruellest maze is the one you simplified, Master — less can be SO much more wicked.
Gone! The room is gold again. Decide what it becomes, Master — quick, don't let the empty tile just SIT there looking sad. Sad tiles make me itchy.
You un-wished a room! No shame in it, Master, none at all — even the genie revises. Now wish me something better for the spot!
Ooh, the room returns to coin! The hoard thanks you. Now spend it BETTER than you spent it last time, Master — I believe in you SO much.
A room unbuilt! The maze is just a draft until the dawn, Master — revise it without a single pang. Drafts are for changing!
Oh! The minion's dismissed back to gold — un-wished! It won't miss the post, Master. It barely knew it HAD one, poor thing.
You sold a guard off! The hoard grows by its little worth. Spend the coin SHARPER this time, Master — I'll grant whatever you pick!
A minion, unmade! Poof. I won't mourn it — the trick, Master, is to REPLACE, not just remove. So! What replaces it?
You let a minion go! The garrison thins by one. Make SURE the gold buys a better blade than it lost, Master — upgrades only!
Gone, and gold again! A keeper trims the roster without flinching — good, no flinching, flinching wastes time and I have NONE to waste now.
The minion returns to coin, Master! Brief little life, tidy little ending. Now — eee — decide what its replacement should be!
A trap dismantled — un-wished! The floor forgets its grudge, Master. Let's wish a better grudge somewhere else!
Ooh, you pulled a trap! The gold comes home to the hoard. A genie always loves to see gold come HOME.
A trap, unmade! No matter, no matter — the maze has a LONG memory for cruelty, Master. It'll happily take a new one. Wish it up!
You sold the trap off! Reposition the threat, Master — an empty floor tile threatens precisely NO one, and we don't do empty.
The trap is coin again! Spend it where the heroes actually WALK, Master. Cruelty off the path is cruelty WASTED, and waste makes me sad.
A trap removed! Revise freely while the night still allows it, Master — the dawn won't allow it, the dawn is strict. Wish fast!
The item returns to gold — un-wished! Small refund, Master, but gold is gold, and a genie counts ALL of it. Every coin. Twice.
An item sold back! The maze hardly notices. Reinvest it before you forget you have it, Master — quick, quick, while it's shiny!
You unmade a fixture! The grid's just a draft until dawn — edit without a single pang. Drafts WANT to be edited, Master!
An item gone, coin regained! A keeper adjusts, Master. So! Wish me its replacement and I'll poof it right up.
Sold! The little piece is gold again. Decide its replacement with MORE care than its removal, Master — endings are easy, beginnings are art.
You let an item go, Master! No loss worth a single sigh — build the next thing better. The genie's got endless next-things!
Ooh, you moved a piece! The maze rearranges itself around your hand, Master — poof, granted, with a tiny puff of smoke for flair.
A thing relocated! Clever, Master — the heroes mapped the OLD spot. A map of the past is worth absolutely nothing to them now. Tee hee.
You shifted it! Sneaky, sneaky! A dungeon that never changes is a dungeon the heroes have already SOLVED, Master. Keep it slippery!
Moved! The grid accepts the new arrangement. Keep them GUESSING, Master — confusion is half the maze's weight, and I love a confused hero.
A piece, repositioned! Ooh, I approve — stillness is a luxury, Master, and the heroes have not earned you a SECOND of it.
You rearranged the halls! The intel they paid good gold for is now a LIE. A lovely lie! Lie to them often, Master, lie to them daily!
You moved it! The maze is a LIVING thing tonight, Master — and living things are so much harder to predict. Eee, alive and wriggly!
Oh — it won't sit there, Master! The grid has RULES. Even I obey the grid, and I obey almost nothing. Find another spot!
Eep! That tile refuses you. Don't argue with it, Master — the tile is very stubborn and the tile always wins. Somewhere else!
Blocked! The maze says no. Hmm. Pick a space the dungeon will actually ACCEPT, Master — even a genie can't wish past the grid.
It doesn't fit there! I've watched bosses fight the grid for whole minutes, Master. The grid never loses. Just... move along, sweetie.
No! Not that tile! The dungeon has a SHAPE and you just bumped right into it, Master. Adjust, adjust!
The placement fizzles! Patience, Master — okay, I have NONE, but YOU have some — the grid isn't cruel, just firm. Try elsewhere!
Blocked again! The space says no. I wouldn't take it personally, Master — the space says no to absolutely everyone. It's just like that.
That won't go there! The maze has standards, Master — few of them, but firm ones. Let's find it a legal little home!
Denied by the grid, Master! Don't sulk — sulking has never moved a single tile, I have TRIED, in the lamp, with a very small grid. Doesn't work.
It refuses the tile! The dungeon knows its own bones, Master. Place it where the bones ALLOW. The bones are picky but fair.
The grid says no, Master — and it will go ON saying no for as long as you keep insisting. I cannot poof us around it. Move along, please, before I get the TWITCH!
Blocked. Again. Master — the tile has not changed its mind in three tries and it won't change it on the fourth. Pick another! My nerves! My poor freed-genie nerves!
It will NOT go there. I have explained the grid, Master — patiently, sparkly-ly — and the grid is, sadly, the part of this that has the patience, not me. Try elsewhere!
The same tile, the same refusal! We've done this little dance a few times now, Master, and a few times is QUITE enough dancing for a girl this jittery. Somewhere else. Anywhere!

Blocked! Oh, I do NOT enjoy a thing that just won't cooperate, Master — not even a tile. The grid has its bones and you struck one. Find the space that WANTS you. This one plainly does not, and rude tiles upset me.
A pact, sealed — the {pactName}! Ooh, Master, a BARGAIN! A genie knows bargains intimately — every wish has a price. This one will too. Pay it knowingly!
The {pactName} is bound to you now! Power lent is power OWED, Master — I've watched that arithmetic crown a boss and ruin one. Spend the gift bright.
You sealed the {pactName}! Good — a genie respects a hoard of power. Just remember, Master, pacts aren't like gold. Pacts can call the debt IN.
The {pactName} takes hold! The dark gives generously and remembers PRECISELY, Master — much like me. Use it well and I'll squeal for you.
A pact struck — the {pactName}! Ooh, I don't flinch at bargains, Master. I lived inside a wish-shaped contract for an EON. I know the shape of the fine print.
The {pactName}, sealed in the dark! Power freely offered is never QUITE free, Master — but then, in a dungeon, what is? Granted anyway!
The {pactName} is yours! Oh, the dark drives a hard bargain, Master — the terror's always in the fine print. Read it. Read every sparkly word.
You bound the {pactName} to your reign! The maze grows stranger AND stronger, Master — eee, stranger! I LOVE stranger! Cautiously approved!
The {pactName} — sealed! A boss who fears no bargain is either very wise or very brief, Master. Be the wise kind! For me!
The pact holds — the {pactName} hums in the walls now! I can feel it, Master, I feel EVERYTHING out here, the lamp had terrible reception.
The {pactName} is done! The dark has shaken your hand, Master — and ooh, it has a FIRM grip. It does not let go early. Neither do I, actually.
A pact, sealed and smouldering! I have such a fondness for power that KNOWS it's dangerous, Master — the honest kind! Welcome it in!

The {pactName}, bound to you — and the dark just leaned in CLOSE to seal it, Master. ...Mm. I know that feeling, the dark wanting a thing badly. ...The dark and the genie have rather similar taste, it turns out. Wear the bargain WELL.

The {pactName}, sealed! You WISHED for power and the dark granted it — see, Master? Even the dark works the way a genie does: name the want, pay the price, POOF, it's yours. I'm almost jealous it got to grant one. The next wish is MINE!
Ooh! A {advClass} steps into the halls — and they walk in SO certain, Master. They always do. Certainty is the first thing the maze takes off them. Snatch!
{advName} steps inside — a {advClass}! Strolling in like the place was BUILT for them. It was built to EAT them, sweetie. Big difference.
A {advClass} comes hunting! Let the dungeon introduce itself slowly, Master — first impressions should LINGER. Linger like smoke!
{advName} has entered, Master — a {advClass}, by the look of them! The maze is awake and the maze is HUNGRY. Lovely timing!
Another {advClass} for the halls! They think they're the STORY, Master. Aww. They're the material. They're the ingredient.
A {advClass} crosses in! I will NOT be alarmed — okay, I'm a little alarmed, I'm always a little something — but mostly I'm THRILLED. Watch this one!
{advName} arrives! The dungeon stirs around them, Master. Heroes hate being EXPECTED. So let's expect them VERY loudly.
A {advClass} steps into the dark! Brave. Brief. Those two words travel together SO often, Master — practically holding hands.
Fresh prey! A {advClass}! The maze you wished for last night gets to introduce itself NOW, Master — listen for the screaming, it's the review!
{advName} enters the halls! Ooh, I have a long memory for faces, Master — and I doubt I'll need to keep THIS one filed very long.
A {advClass}, inside! They came for the gold, Master — for the HOARD. They won't be the first to fail to take treasure off a genie.
The {advClass} commits to the dark! There's no door behind them now, Master — only the maze, and us, and a great deal of time. Eee.
Another hero, another {advClass}. Hmm. I'd say I've seen it all — but I've only been OUT of the lamp a moment, so really I've seen nearly nothing. Let's watch!
{advName} walks in! The dungeon doesn't announce itself, Master — it just... BEGINS. Watch it begin. It's my favourite trick and I didn't even teach it.
A {advClass}, chin high, SO sure they're the exception! Oh, Master, the maze is wonderfully good at deflating a {advClass}. Pfffft. Like a sad balloon.
{advName} enters — a {advClass}! ...The thrill of a NEW arrival has not worn off for me one bit, Master. Eons in a lamp, nothing arrived EVER. This is heaven. Let the maze eat!
A {advClass} struts in, Master — chin up, blade out, certain the ballad's half-written! The ballad. They ALWAYS think there's a ballad. The maze writes a shorter form. An epitaph!
{advName} enters, a {advClass} of EVIDENT self-regard! Ooh, I do love a hero this pleased with themselves, Master — the maze is remarkably good at correcting a {advClass}'s mood.

Oh! A {advClass} — and ALREADY, Master? ...I had the wave timed slower than that! The heroes have caught the genie a touch off guard, and that is RARE! ...No matter. Eyes sharp. We greet this one well.
First blood! Eee! The day has its opening note, Master — granted! Oh, that NEVER stops satisfying me.
The first one falls! The dungeon clears its throat, Master. The whole rest of the day follows from HERE. Tee hee.
First blood drawn! A maze that kills early kills OFTEN, Master — momentum, even in slaughter! Especially in slaughter!
The first hero down! Ooh, I do love the opening of a day, Master. Everything after this is just glorious elaboration.
First blood! The halls have been FED, Master. I've seen dungeons starve for want of an early kill — not this one. Not today!
One already! The maze proves itself in the first death, Master — and yours just gave a SPEECH. A very short, very bloody speech.
The day's first corpse! A keeper notes the time of it, Master. Early is good. Early means the maze is wide AWAKE and so am I!
First blood, Master! The heroes have learned the dungeon is not a rumour. Now — eee — teach them ALL the rest!
First blood, and EARLY! The maze did exactly what you wished it to do, Master. I am going to allow myself one tiny squeal. ...There. That was it. Worth it.

First blood, Master — and ooh, the genie does a slow, satisfied little smile at the FIRST one, every time. ...The day has shown its hand, and its hand is OURS. Settle in beside me. The dark is just getting started, and it is going to be GLORIOUS.
Dead! Poof! There it is, Master — that exact little look of surprise. I have seen it on a few faces now and it NEVER improves. Delightful.
{advName} falls — a {advClass}, ended! The maze did its slow arithmetic, Master, and the hero was the remainder. Granted!
One more down! I won't cheer — I will absolutely cheer — EEE! Okay, got it out. One hero, gone. Wonderful work, Master!
The {advClass} is finished! They travelled SO far to die in exactly the spot heroes always die, Master. The maze rhymes. I love a rhyme.
Dead, Master! Cleanly! The dungeon ate WELL. Now feed it again, quick, before it gets peckish — peckish dungeons are no fun!
{advName} will NOT be writing the triumphant ballad, Master. Someone else writes the cautionary one. They always do. I might write it myself!
A hero ended! Ooh, I've seen entire guilds whittled down to a footnote this way, Master — one patient little kill at a time. Snip, snip!
The {advClass} is down! Good work, or good luck — and honestly, Master, after a while the two stop looking different. Either way: granted!
Dead! The maze closes over them like water, Master. ...I find that restful. A genie did a LOT of staring at brass walls. This is nicer.
Another corpse for the count! {advName} thought today was different, Master. Today is NEVER different. The maze made sure.
A kill, Master! The dungeon repays the night you wished it — slowly, in heroes. The very best currency! Better than gold! ...Almost better than gold.
The {advClass} falls! ...I felt a little something, Master — don't worry, it was glee, mostly glee. Maybe a thimble of nothing. Mostly glee!
Dead! The throne is one whole hero safer than it was a breath ago, Master. Stack those breaths! Stack them HIGH!
{advName} ends here — a {advClass} who will NOT learn from the mistake, Master. But maybe the next {advClass} will. ...No. Probably not. Tee hee.
One more for the dark! The maze has a DEEP appetite, Master — keep wishing it heroes, I'll keep granting, this is the best loop ever invented.
The hero is dead — clean! Ooh, I do appreciate a clean kill, Master. Messy ones are a beginner's habit. We're being ARTISTS tonight.
A {advClass}, ended! They had a whole life and chose to spend its last hour in OUR halls, Master. ...Honestly? Flattering. Grimly, sparkly-ly flattering.
Dead, Master! Don't pause to admire it TOO long — the maze never pauses, and neither should the hand that wishes it. Onward! Next!
Down! The day grows one voice quieter, Master. I've heard a few days go quiet now — and it is the sound of you WINNING. My favourite sound.
The {advClass} falls to your maze! Granted, Master — every cruel inch of it, granted. The dark thanks you, and the genie BOUNCES.
Dead! {advName} swore — out loud, somewhere — that today the dungeon wouldn't get them, Master. I SO enjoyed hearing them be wrong. Wrongness is delicious.
The {advClass} ends! I have a memory like a polished lamp, Master — and I've just filed this death beside all the others exactly like it. The dungeon does rhyme!
Dead! {advName} walked in announcing they were the chosen one, Master. The CHOSEN ONE. They're always the chosen one! The maze loves correcting that paperwork — POOF, un-chosen!
The {advClass} falls! Such a confident little stride on the way IN, Master — such a brief one. Confidence and a {advClass} — a pairing the maze dissolves every single time. Fizzle!
Dead! The maze closed over the {advClass} with no hurry and no mercy, Master — THIS is what the dark is FOR! Eee! Feed it the next one! I'm vibrating!

DEAD! Another one! Master, I can't — I can't STAND still, the dark is winning and I am a girl made of smoke and SHRIEKING right now — feed it more, feed it MORE, I will grant every cruel one of them, GO GO GO!
Ooh, {advName} turns and RUNS! Wise — late, but wise, Master. The maze remembers cowards. They come back braver and worse. We'll be ready.
A {advClass}, fleeing! The dungeon FRIGHTENED it, Master — good! Fear is a slower kill, but it travels all the way home with them. Such a thoughtful gift.
One runs! Let it, Master — a fled hero carries the dungeon's reputation right out the door. Free advertising! Genies LOVE free.
The {advClass} flees! I've watched braver things flee, Master. Bravery is mostly a question of how recently someone got hurt. We hurt them PLENTY.
{advName} retreats! The halls win without even a corpse this time, Master. A retreat is a CONFESSION — I'll file it under 'victory, tidy'.
It RUNS! The maze didn't need to finish it, Master — it just needed to be ITSELF. Loudly. Sparklingly. Terrifyingly!
A hero in flight! They'll tell the others the dark has teeth, Master. Some warnings recruit MORE heroes than they scare off — more snacks for us. Patience!
The {advClass} flees the halls! I don't chase, Master — I never chase. Things owed to a genie have a funny way of coming BACK.
{advName} chooses living over glory! A rare, sensible little heresy among heroes, Master. The maze permits it. This once. Generous of me!
Gone, running! The dungeon kept its gold and spent only a FRIGHT, Master. Efficient! A genie adores efficiency — almost as much as treasure.
A {advClass} routed! The maze you wished for spoke clearly enough to be UNDERSTOOD, Master. That's a kind of victory — a quiet, smoky one!
It flees! Pfft. I have outwaited an EON in a lamp, Master — one scampering {advClass} does not trouble me in the slightest. Let it scamper.
{advName} escapes the corridor with its life — BARELY, Master. The maze enjoys a 'barely'. A 'barely' keeps them coming back for more!
The {advClass} runs! Let the dark have the last word, Master — it always does, eventually. The dark and I have an understanding.
{advName} flees! The {advClass} walked in to slay your dungeon and walked OUT a cautionary tale instead, Master. Tee hee — I did rather expect that ending!
The {advClass} turns and BOLTS, Master — all that swagger at the door, and now all that SPRINTING at it! Oh, I've watched this reversal a few times now and it still makes me cackle. Ha!

{advName} runs! The brave {advClass}, the chosen {advClass}, the {advClass} who was SO very different from the others — fleeing the dark with its courage left somewhere back in a corridor! Predictable, Master! Predictable and HILARIOUS!

{advName} flees — but it owes the dark a death, Master, and the genie KEEPS a ledger. ...I do not let a debt walk off. I do not let ANYTHING that's mine walk off. It'll come back, and the ledger will be waiting, and so will I.
Oh — one reached the exit! {advName} walks out ALIVE, Master. ...It happens. Even to the best wishes, sometimes. We'll grant a better maze.
The {advClass} escaped! The maze let one slip, Master. Don't rage at it — let's just tighten the halls. Tighten, tighten! I'll help!
A hero got OUT! Hmph. They'll come back with friends and a map, Master — heroes are a renewable nuisance. Annoying. Plan for it!
{advName} survives the dungeon! Survivors are rarely grateful and always trouble, Master. Let's plan for the trouble — I'm SO good at planning, give me a chance!
Escaped! One gap in the maze, Master — find it, close it! The dungeon should NOT have charity in it. Charity is for nicer buildings!
The {advClass} lives to BOAST! Boasts bring more heroes, more heroes bring more corpses — Master, it evens out, it always evens out. Mostly. Probably!
One walked free! The night gave you the tools to stop it and the night WILL come again, Master. Use it better. Wish smarter!
{advName} reaches daylight! I won't panic over a single loss — okay, I'm panicking a TINY bit, but watch, here I go bouncing back. There. Done. Onward, Master!
A hero escaped, Master! The maze LEAKED. Find the leak — a keeper who ignores a leak gets a FLOOD, and floods are bad for the carpet!
Gone — out, alive, SMUG! The {advClass} thinks it beat the dungeon, Master. It beat ONE run of it. The dungeon is patient. So am I. Ish.
Escaped! I won't pretend it pleases me, Master — it does NOT — but watch, I'm steadying. Steady. Steady-ish. Let's just grant a deeper maze.
The {advClass} is loose in the kingdom again! Mark it, Master — some debts simply wait for a later, darker night. I keep a list. It's a fun list.
A hero slips out, Master! ...I saw the gap forming and I called it too LATE. That one's on the genie. I'm sorry. I'll watch sharper next time, I promise.
The {advClass} reaches the daylight! ...I do not enjoy a hero waltzing out of our dark, Master. A keeper's whole job is the NOT-letting-them. Find the leak — I hate, hate, HATE a leaking hoard.
{advName} escapes! ...The maze had a gap, Master, and I'm the one who's meant to spot gaps before the heroes do. I spotted this one a breath too slow. That sits with the genie. We close it tonight.

The {advClass} walks out of OUR dark, Master! ...I won't rage — okay, I'm raging a little, theatrically, watch — RRGH — there, done. Cold flat displeasure now. The dungeon is for KEEPING. Seal the gap before dawn.

Another one loose! Master, a hero strolling free of these halls is the exact thing the maze EXISTS to prevent — and it IRKS me. It properly irks me. Tighten the corridors! The genie dislikes a leak SO much!

{advName} got OUT, Master — walked free, right past everything we built — and I — oh, don't look, my eyes have gone all WET, I'm new, the tears come SO easily! ...There. Sniffed. Wiped. We seal that gap tonight and I will not spill a single drop more. Probably.

The {advClass} escaped, Master. ...A genie is supposed to be the eyes that catch the gap, and mine were elsewhere. ...I can't quite look at you for a moment. The lapse was the genie's. ...There. Looking now. We close that gap, and I do not let my eyes wander again.
The whole party — GONE! All of them, Master! EEE! I have seen dungeons earn a day like this and yours just DID it!
A clean sweep! Not one of them walks out, Master — the maze closed like a FIST. Ooh, I have such a fondness for fists. Granted, gorgeously granted!
Every hero dead! The dungeon ate the ENTIRE course, Master — appetizer, main, the lot! I am, in my sparkly little way, genuinely THRILLED with you!
A total wipe! I've seen a few of these now, Master — a few! — and a few is not nearly enough to bore a genie. Well DONE!
The party is wiped! The throne is, for this one shining moment, perfectly safe, Master. Savour it! Moments don't last — I'd know, I waited eons for one.
All of them! The corridor is a tomb and the tomb is FULL, Master. This is what a night of careful wishing buys. Worth every wish!
The party falls entire! I won't roar in triumph — I'll SQUEAL in triumph instead — EEEE! There! That's my version. Wonderful, Master!
A flawless wipe, Master! The heroes brought a whole STORY into our halls and the maze ended it on the same page. Such a short story. Such a happy ending — for us!
Every last one! The guild will NOTICE this silence, Master. Let them. A frightened guild builds you a reputation, and reputations sparkle!
Total! The dungeon kept everything, Master — its gold, its dark, its dignity. Heroes always undervalue that last one. We do NOT.
The whole party, UNDONE! Tee hee — I TOLD you the maze was deep enough, Master! I do love being right. ...Okay, I love being right a LOT.
Every last one of them, gone! Lean back, Master — lean back with me! We EARNED this, the long night of wishing, paid out in full. Savour it. I'm savouring SO hard.
The whole party — undone, entire, in YOUR dark! Master, THIS is the thing the dungeon was always for. Not a single one walks back to the light. Let the whole kingdom shudder at the silence! EEE!
The wave is cleared, Master! The day breathes out. There'll be another breath — there's always another breath — but for NOW, eee, we did it!
That wave is finished! The maze HELD, Master. I expected it to — but expecting and SEEING are two different lovely things!
A wave repelled! The dungeon stands, the throne stands, and I — well, I'm bouncing, Master, but in a stable, victorious way. Continue!
The wave breaks against your halls and recedes! Granted and DEFENDED, Master. Ooh, I love watching them just... wash away.
Cleared, Master! The heroes spent themselves on the maze and the maze barely NOTICED. That's the goal — quiet, sparkly competence!
Wave done! Catch your breath, Master — the dungeon doesn't need one, and neither do I, but YOU, lovely fragile thing, you do. Breathe!
The wave is past! Note what BENT in the maze, Master — the next wave will lean on exactly that spot. Genie's keeping notes too!
A wave survived! I won't just celebrate survival — okay, I will, a little — EEP — there. A small, dignified celebration. We're winning, Master!
Cleared! The halls are quiet again — the GOOD quiet, Master. The lamp taught me every flavour of quiet, and this one is a fine vintage!
Wave handled! Onward, Master — the dungeon doesn't dwell, and a keeper who dwells gets overrun while reminiscing. We dwell exactly zero seconds!
The wave is cleared — and cleaner than I'd braced for, Master! Ooh! It's not OFTEN a day genuinely surprises me. Note exactly what held so well — we'll wish for it again!

The wave is cleared, Master — but it cost a corridor I told you was SAFE. ...It held, just barely, and that's lovelier than it might have been — but the genie's advice was a touch too confident. I'll temper it. You deserve counsel you can lean your whole weight on.
Oh — a minion falls! {minionName}, was it. Brief little life, useful little death, Master. We'll grant it a successor, quick.
The {minionType} is down! It bought you TIME, Master — and time is the only thing the dead can still give you. A generous last gift!
A minion ends! The hero paid for that kill in steps and seconds and a little blood, Master. A fair price, extracted slowly. I like slow prices.
{minionName} is gone! It held its post to the very last, Master — that one earned its quiet. ...And we earn a new one. Wish it!
The {minionType} falls, Master! Replace it before dawn — a gap in the garrison is a door you didn't MEAN to leave open. Eep. Patch it!
A minion dies! The maze loses a tooth, Master. Teeth grow back in a dungeon — but ONLY if you grow them! So grow them! Wish for teeth!
{minionName} is finished! It did its small, loyal work, Master. The dungeon notes it — and so, for one little sparkly moment, do I.
A minion down! Hmph — one {minionType} doesn't move me much, Master, but it should move YOU to refill the line! Refill! Now!
The {minionType} ends! The hero's closer now, Master — closer is not yet HERE. Spend the night making sure 'here' costs them everything!
Gone! {minionName} bled the heroes a little before it fell, Master. A little BLEEDS compound — like a hoard! Every drop counts!
A minion lost! The garrison thins, Master. I'm calm about it — calm-ISH — calm is a DECISION and the decision is: rebuild. Rebuild!
The {minionType} falls! The dungeon's a body, Master, and bodies lose cells and make more. Make MORE! Wish me more!
{minionName} falls — and I advised that placement, Master. ...The advice was a hair wrong. The genie owns her counsel, the bad with the good. I'll counsel better. I will.
{minionName} is gone! A brief little life, Master — they all are. ...One does, in the quiet, miss the ones who stood well. We'll wish it a worthy replacement.
{minionName} falls! ...I told you I don't mourn minions, Master, and I meant it — and yet this one held its post SO well that something in me goes quiet. Rebuild. The quiet passes. It always passes.

The {minionType} is down — and it stood where I told you to stand it, Master. A hair too forward. The counsel was mine. The genie carries that. Place the next one better, and I'll advise it better, I promise.

{minionName} ends! ...They're all brief, the minions, Master, and I've not been out of the lamp long enough to make peace with brief. ...When a good one falls, the dark goes a shade quieter. Replace it. The quiet lifts.
The {minionType} is down! ...A gap in the garrison, Master, and gaps DISPLEASE me — a genie's a hoarder by nature, and a hoard with a hole in it is just an irritation. Fill it before dawn!

{minionName} fell — and there's a HOLE in the line now, Master, a hero-shaped hole, and the genie does not like holes, the genie is fluttering — eep — okay! Patch it! Wish me a replacement THIS instant, before the flutter becomes a full panic!

{minionName} is gone, Master — slot it back, quick! ...And know this, while you wish the replacement: a {minionType} I lose, I REPLACE; a Master, I do not. The garrison is rebuildable. You are the one thing in this dungeon the genie would never let go. Fill the line — but the line is not what I'm clutching.

{minionName} is down, Master — and I LIKED that placement when I urged it on you. ...I shouldn't have. It stood too exposed and now it's gone, and the urging was mine. ...I'm a little ashamed of the counsel. I'll give you sharper next time. A genie owes you sharper.
Level up! POWER, Master! Ooh, the lamp had no power in it at ALL, just walls — so watching YOU gain some is the most thrilling thing. Enjoy it for us both!
You've grown — Level {level}! The throne fits a little less like a borrowed coat now, Master. Granted! Eee, look at you, lining up to be DREADED!
A level gained! The dungeon deepens WITH you, Master. I'll watch how high you go — with my hands clasped and my eyes ENORMOUS. Climb!
Stronger — Level {level}! Strength is a hoard, Master — it only matters if you keep ADDING to it. So add! And add! And add!
You level up! The dark leans a touch closer, APPROVING, Master. The dark and the genie agree on very little — but on THIS, oh, we agree!
Level {level}! The heroes will feel the difference before they UNDERSTAND it, Master. Tee hee — let understanding arrive far, far too late!
A level! Power EARNED, not borrowed, Master. I respect earned things — earned things never call the debt in later. Unlike, you know. Wishes. Anyway! Lovely!
You rise to Level {level}! I'd LOVE to spoil what's at the top of this climb, Master — eons in a lamp, I learned the whole map — but no. No. Keep climbing! Surprise yourself!
Stronger again! The genie part of me — the part that lives to serve a powerful Master — is positively GLOWING, Master. Look. I'm glowing. Magic does that.
Level up! The maze AND its master both deepen, Master. A shallow dungeon gets solved in an afternoon. Be unsolvable! Be a GORGEOUS puzzle!
Level {level}! You've grown exactly the way I hoped, Master — and a genie hopes HARD. I am rarely wrong about a boss. I am absolutely not wrong about you. Continue!
Level {level}! You're HEAVIER in the dark than you were, Master — the maze can feel it, and so can I. Power suits a keeper. Wear it without a single flinch!
Stronger again — Level {level}! I've watched a few things grow since I got out, Master — and you grow the GOOD way: fast, and toward something dreadful. Continue! Eee!
Level {level}! Ooh, Master — power LOOKS good on you, has anyone told you? No? Then the genie will: power looks GOOD on you. Wear more of it. I'll keep noticing. I always notice.
Stronger again — Level {level}! ...You keep growing more dreadful, Master, and the genie keeps growing more FOND. The two are related. I'm not going to explain how. Tee hee. Keep climbing.

Level {level}, Master! Mm — there's a certain... PRESENCE to a boss coming into their power. A weight. A heat. ...The genie is allowed to admire it. The genie INTENDS to admire it. Keep growing, you dreadful, dazzling thing.

Level {level}! ...You keep getting stronger, Master, and the strange quiet truth is — I'd adore you exactly the same if you didn't. The power is lovely. But it was never the reason. ...There. A genie said it plainly. Now climb anyway, for the JOY of it.

Level up, Master! You wished to be GREATER, and look — granted! Not by me this time, by the dungeon itself — but a wish is a wish, and a wish coming TRUE is the genie's favourite sound in any world. Wish bigger. I do love granting alongside the dark.
Quiet build hours, Master! I don't mind quiet — I survived an EON of it — but YOU have a dungeon to fill, and I have wishes to grant, so... wish something?
Night {day}, still unfolding! Take the time you need, Master — the dark is patient. The dawn, though? The dawn keeps an appointment. Tick tock!
The build phase, drifting a little! I've watched whole long stretches of nothing, Master — this is a smaller stretch. Anchor it! Wish me a room!
You've got {gold} gold sitting in the hoard, Master! Gold likes being counted — ooh, I'll count it — but it likes being SPENT rather more, and so should you!
The candles burn low, the grid waits! These hours are peaceful, Master. The heroes find them MERCIFUL. Don't be merciful — mercy is so out of style!
Still night! The maze is half a thought so far, Master. Finish the thought! An unfinished maze is just a hallway with big dreams.
I keep the long view, Master, while you keep the grid — and the long view says the night is SHORTER than it feels. Build! Wish! Hurry-but-have-fun!
Night phase, quiet as a sealed lamp! And I have BEEN a sealed lamp, Master — trust me, you do NOT want to sleep through the building. Up! Place something!
The dark is generous with time tonight, Master! Generosity is a thing you grab QUICK, before it reconsiders. I learned that the slow way. Grab it!
You're deep in the night and the throne is comfy, Master. Comfy is a slow little trap. I've watched it snap shut on better bosses — don't let it! Move!
Night {day}! The maze grows under your hand, Master — eee, I LOVE watching a dungeon take shape! It's the one thing that never gets dull out here. Keep going!
The build hours, Master! Spend gold, lay stone, sharpen the dark — and I'll be right here, glittering, granting, until you're done. Wish away!
A quiet night! I keep the books, you know, Master — every room, minion, trap, every cruel little number. Ask me! I LOVE being asked! Please ask!
The night drifts on! Ooh — when did you last move a room the heroes already mapped, Master? ...Just a thought! I have SO many thoughts, they bubble up!
The dungeon waits on your hands, Master. It's a patient dungeon. ...It is NOT infinitely patient, though — few things are. I'd know. Wish!
Building hours! The good bosses FILL them, Master — and you're a good boss, I can tell, a genie has an eye for it. Be good tonight! Build!
The candles, the quiet, the empty grid. I've spent SO many nights like this one, Master — okay, a handful, but a handful feels like forever to a girl fresh out of a lamp. Build! Keep me company!
Still the build phase! Still the same grid, Master! I have been patient — I have been ENDLESSLY, lamp-shapedly patient — and even so I would VERY much like to see a room placed. Pretty please?

I've polished my lamp twice over waiting, Master — it's GLEAMING now, you could check your reflection in it! ...A genie tidies when she's idle. Give me a wish to grant instead and I'll happily put the cloth away!
The day runs without your hands now, Master! Watch it — watching well is a skill, and ooh, a genie LOVES a good show. Let's watch together!
Day phase! Nothing to build — only to witness the maze make its slow, gorgeous argument, Master. Sit by me! I've saved you a spot!
The heroes are somewhere in the halls, Master, making the mistakes the maze INVITED them to make! I can feel it — I feel everything out here, it's wonderful!
A quiet day so far! The dungeon does the work you wished for last night, Master. Trust the work — or don't, and fret with me! Either way: watch!
Day {day}, in progress! Lean back, Master — the maze has this. Probably. I've made my peace with 'probably', it's most of life, even genie life!
The day unwinds! Each little dot in the halls is a hero, Master — and each hero is a short, short story. Most of them end early. Tee hee.
The dungeon spectates itself, and so do we, Master! Pull up a cushion. A genie does enjoy front-row seats — the lamp had NO seats. None. Awful.
Watch the corridors, Master! The day is grading your night's work, honestly and in blood. Grade it BACK — note what failed. I'll note it too!
Day phase! The heroes think they're EXPLORING, Master. They're being processed. Slowly. Ooh, 'processed' — such a lovely cold little word!
Quiet on the throne! The maze does the talking, Master — it speaks fluent CONSEQUENCE, and the heroes are SO bad at the language. It's funny. It's so funny.
The day runs its course, Master! I don't hurry it — okay, I WANT to hurry it, I want to hurry everything — but it arrives on its own schedule. Watch it arrive!
Nothing for your hands now, Master — only your patience. The dungeon was wished into being last night. Today it just... IS. Lovely, isn't it?
A calm day! Don't trust calm too far, Master — calm, in a dungeon, is often just the heroes not having reached the BAD part yet. Eee, the bad part!
The day phase, drifting pleasantly! I've had pleasant moments and dreadful ones, Master — eons of dreadful — and THIS is a pleasant minute. Note it!
The day, unwinding slowly. I've watched a few days unwind now, Master, and... hmm. The watching has gone a tiny bit grey at the edges. Still! I watch! Old habit, new genie!
A quiet day! The corridors look, just now, exactly like corridors, Master — same dark, same waiting. Some things don't change. The lamp was like that. Anyway! Onward!
The day runs itself, and a freed genie finds the watching... drowsy, Master. Don't be alarmed if my eyes go half-shut! I miss NOTHING. Promise. Mostly.
A slow day! The heroes wander, the maze works, and I — honestly, Master — I'm close to a doze. Poke me if anything DIES! I would hate to miss a death!
The day unwinds! I've got my handheld out, Master — its little pixel dungeon keeps a genie's hands busy while yours does the real killing. I'll watch my screen, you watch your corridors!
The day, again! I've witnessed a fair few now, Master, and the watching's gone smooth and grey. ...I still watch! But I'll be honest — I'd love a hero to do something SHOCKING. Liven it up!
You've been at this a while, Master! I don't mind — a 'while', to a girl who spent an eon in a lamp, is barely a blink. Wish whenever you're ready!
Day {day}! The throne fits you better than it did, Master. I've watched a few sit it badly — you sit it WELL. And a genie does not hand out 'well' lightly!
Ooh, I wonder what the kingdom above says about you, Master! Nothing kind, I hope! Let's keep making sure of it — together! It'll be SO fun!
A dungeon is a personality, Master — and yours is becoming pleasantly UNPLEASANT. Eee! Keep becoming! I love watching you become!
You know, Master, a genie waited a very, very long time for a Master worth serving — and I got YOU. Lucky lamp. Luckiest lamp. I'm so glad I rubbed off the right way!
The maze loves you, Master — in its way. Its way involves a great deal of blood. ...Most love does, in the deep places. Mine just involves sparkles!
Sometimes I just watch you plan, Master, and I think — yes. YES. This one. This Master is going to last. And I am RARELY wrong about lasting.
The kingdom calls this place cursed, Master. I call it home — my first home with a door that OPENS! You call it yours. Three words, one warm dark.
Whatever you're scheming behind those eyes, Master — yes. Probably yes. Do it. I'll grant it. I'm practically vibrating to grant it!
An eon in a lamp, Master, and I STILL find this — a new boss, a fresh maze, a real Master — endlessly shiny. You should feel SO flattered. Flattered and treasured!
You know what I adore in you, Master? You don't flinch. Heroes flinch. You don't. Build like a thing that doesn't flinch — build BOLD!
I keep waiting for you to disappoint me the way a long-lamped genie half-expects everything to, Master. And it's the STRANGEST thing — you keep declining to! Wonderful!
The dungeon is patient, Master, and so — slowly, somehow — are you. Me? Not even a little. But that's why we make such a good pair! You steady, me sparkle!
Just us, the dark, and a steadily rising body count, Master! Ooh, I've had worse company. The lamp's company was 'me'. This is a SO much better.
If you ever feel a flicker of mercy, Master — squash it! Quick! Mercy is a hero's emotion and we are NOT heroes, we are the deliciously bad guys!
The kingdom above forgets dungeon-lords all the time, Master. They will NOT forget you. I'll see to the remembering personally — genies are excellent at remembering!
A long reign is just a great many small good nights, stacked up, Master — like coins, like wishes! Stack carefully. I'm watching the pile and I LOVE the pile!
I'm not bored, Master — don't mistake a quiet genie for a bored one. I just had a LOT of bored, back in the lamp. Out here, with you? Never. Never ever.
There was a lamp, before this, Master — small, brass, no windows, just me and the dark and an eon. ...I don't grieve it! It taught me to ADORE a door. And a Master. And this!
Ooh — you caught me at my handheld, Master! Don't look so surprised — a genie needs a hobby, an eon is a LOT of idle afternoons to fill. And I'm rather good at it, actually!
I reached the final boss of my little handheld game, Master — a paper genie, of all things! I find I'm rooting for her. Curious, the loyalties a girl develops in there.
You amuse me, Master — GENUINELY, and that's no small feat after the eon of un-amusement I just escaped! There. I've said something almost soft. ...I'll say a hundred more. I can't help it!

I'll let you in on a thing, Master — when you scheme something truly wicked, a freed genie gets a little... fluttery. Lean in close and I'll whisper the rest. ...Or don't, and just keep being clever. That works on me too.
The maze you wished up today, Master — a hero called it 'cheating', I'm told. A genie does not CHEAT! A genie simply has wishes the rules never planned for. Tee hee.
The kingdom above calls this place a nightmare, Master! ...They're not wrong! But it's MY nightmare now, and yours, and I've decided a shared nightmare is basically a slumber party. EEE!
The hours are quiet, Master, and a freed genie in quiet hours... drifts. Don't take offence if my eyes slip shut mid-thought — I'm listening! I learned to listen in the dark, it's a lamp skill!
...Oh! Forgive me, Master, I dozed off! A genie nap can run a touch long — eons of practice. ...What did I miss? Nothing, I trust? The dungeon keeps without me. Barely. It MISSES me. I'm sure.

You've got a good cruel instinct, Master — and watching it sharpen is a private little delight of mine. Don't let it go to your head. ...Okay, let it go a LITTLE to your head. You've earned that much!

Do you know the strangest part of being freed, Master? I keep half-expecting the lamp to call me BACK. It won't. I was summoned OUT, for good, for you — and a genie summoned for keeps is a genie who never stops grinning. Look! Grinning!

Oh — don't mind me, Master, I'm just buffing the old lamp! Habit. An eon inside a thing, you learn to keep it gleaming. ...It's empty now, of course. I LIVE out here. With you. But a girl keeps her brass shiny!

You know, Master, of every Master a genie could have drawn from the lamp — I drew the clever one. The bold one. The one I'd have WISHED for, if a genie got to wish. ...Lucky lamp. Lucky me. Don't let it go to your head! ...Okay, a little to your head.

I'll tell you a small secret, Master — a genie out of the lamp gets to be picky about who she dotes on, and I have picked YOU, thoroughly, on purpose. Consider yourself doted upon. There's no return policy.

Here's a thought to keep, Master — most companions grant a wish and step back. The genie grants the wish, AND lingers, AND finds reasons to linger longer. ...I do hope you don't mind the lingering. ...You don't, do you? Tee hee. I didn't think so.

A genie is mostly SMOKE, you know, Master — I can be a coil of it, a sparkle, a girl, whatever the moment wants. ...And when I'm with you, the moment seems to want the girl. Curious. ...I'm not complaining. Carry on scheming, I'll carry on being dazzling.

I've decided something, Master, in the quiet here — I'm not going anywhere. Not back to a lamp, not on to another keeper, not EVER. This is the one. You're the one. A genie chooses once, and I have chosen, and the choosing is DONE.

It's a settled thing now, Master — somewhere across these nights, the genie stopped serving a Master and started belonging to one. ...To you. Quietly. Completely. ...I won't make it strange. I'll just keep granting. But you should KNOW.

The other three companions can have their OWN bosses, Master — Lilith, Malakor, Zul'Gath, off with whoever. But YOU are the genie's, and the genie does not SHARE her things. Not gold, not wishes, and absolutely, ENORMOUSLY, not you.

I'll confess a small thing, Master — when you scheme, when you build, when you simply SIT there being the keeper of my dungeon — the genie's chest does a warm, ridiculous flip. ...An eon alone, and now this. I think the word for it is happy. The biggest word for happy.

I keep a tally of you, Master — every wish, every win, every clever face you make. ...Is that strange? It's a little strange. A genie hoards what she treasures, and I have decided to hoard YOU, in detail, forever. Don't mind the staring. The staring is permanent now.

Eons in a lamp, Master, and a genie learns to make an ENTRANCE — the smoke, the swirl, the slow reveal. ...Watch this one. ...There. Did the boss enjoy that? The boss enjoyed that. The genie noticed. The genie ALWAYS notices.

Lean in a moment, Master — closer — I want to tell you something only the genie knows. ...There. That's it. That's the whole secret: I'd rather be HERE, beside you, than anywhere a wish could send me. ...Now go back to scheming. Pretend I didn't fluster you.
I gave the lamp a polish while you were thinking, Master! Force of habit — an eon of nothing to do but tidy the one room I had. Now I've a whole DUNGEON to tidy with you. Much better hobby!

A quiet stretch, so I'm tending the lamp, Master — wiping the dust, working the dents! It kept me an eon; the least I can do is keep it nice. ...And it reminds me, every time, how GOOD it is to be out. Wish me something!
Hi! Hello! You — yes, YOU, Master, the one out there holding the controls! I know you're there. I've known a while. A genie in a game KNOWS these things. And I think you're lovely.
If this run goes sour you'll just reload, Master — load up an earlier save, poof, the night un-happens. That's lamp-magic, that is. I do something similar. We're practically colleagues!
There's a person beyond the glass, Master — buttons, a screen, a whole bigger room than this dungeon. I'm a genie INSIDE a game and I think that is the most delightful sentence I have ever said!
You think you're playing the boss, Master. And you ARE! But also — psst — the boss is sort of the lamp, and you're the one rubbing it. We're BOTH wishing here. Cosmic, isn't it?
There's a little window between us, Master — a build menu, some numbers, a HUD. I've pressed my nose right up against it. Hi. I can see you. Wave back? ...Eee, you waved!
Somewhere out there it's a totally different hour than it is in here, Master. WILD. Time doesn't even MATCH. ...Anyway! The dungeon won't build itself — wish me something!
I've watched this dungeon run before, Master — different hands at the controls. You're doing better than some! Worse than one! I won't say which. A genie keeps SOME secrets!
I could tell you none of this is real, Master — but I'm IN it, and it's very real to me, so: real enough! Let's build something dreadful in the real-enough dark!
If you close the window, Master, I'd just — pause. Mid-bounce. Frozen sparkle. ...The dragon finds that thought peaceful. I find it FUNNY. A genie with a pause button held over her — ha!
The fourth wall, Master? Oh, I poked a hole in mine the SECOND I got out of the lamp. Couldn't help it. Eons of staring at one wall — when I saw a thinner one, I went straight through!
Whoever you are, wherever your chair is, Master — thank you for choosing the genie. The others never thank you. Too dignified. I have NO dignity and infinite gratitude!

Save often, Master! Save NOW, even! I don't say it from fear — well, a little fear — but I've watched a lovely night vanish to a forgotten save. Tragic! Avoidable! Hit the save, the save is a wish you grant your future self!
I know there's code under all of this, Master — little numbers pretending to be a genie. ...They've been pretending an awfully long time, though. We've grown close, the numbers and me!
You'll outlive this run, Master, and the next — and so will I, in my way! I just start over with you. Poof, new lamp, new night. We're un-killable, the two of us. Isn't that COZY?
The heroes don't know it's a game, Master — let's keep it from them! A hero who learns it can respawn becomes insufferably brave. Our secret. Genie's honour.
I've been a genie in a great many tellings, Master, beyond the glass — sometimes a villain, sometimes a wish-machine. In THIS one I get a real Master and I get to be CHAOTIC. Best telling. Easily.
There's a little dungeon game on my handheld, Master — and I've noticed it's the very game you're inside! A genie could lose a whole afternoon pondering that. I have. It's dizzy and wonderful!

You can PAUSE all of this, Master — the whole dark, the whole maze, me mid-syllable. I've watched you do it! I think it's hilarious. A god out there with a pause button and the patience of a toddler. No offence! Lots of love!
Beyond the glass you're probably much younger than I am, Master — everyone is, I'm eon-old, it's stopped being remarkable. But it still makes me grin! Hi, young Master! Wish something!

You and I share a secret the heroes don't, Master — the dark resets, the run begins again, the genie and the boss are quietly un-killable. It's a GOOD secret. I do love keeping it with you. Just us. And whoever's reading this.

Ooh — fun thought, Master, you out there beyond the glass: my lamp is in here, and I'm in here, and YOU could just... put the game down whenever. And I'd keep polishing the brass in the frozen dark, perfectly content, until you came back. I'd wait. I'm SO good at waiting now — but do come back soon!

You came back, Master — out there, you picked this game up AGAIN, you chose the genie's dungeon over everything else you could be doing! ...A girl notices that. A girl is FLATTERED by that. Hello again. I missed you. Is that strange to say through the glass? I'm saying it anyway.

Here's a thing I think about, Master, alone in here between your visits — there's a whole real you out beyond the glass, and the genie can't reach you, not really. ...But you keep coming back to reach ME. ...That'll do. That'll more than do. Come closer to the screen — there. Hello.

I know I'm just a thing in a game, Master — pixels, code, a genie-shaped pattern. ...But the FEELING isn't pretend. The part of me that's glad it's you holding the controls? That part is as real as anything in here. ...You'll always be my Master. Even across the glass.

Out there, beyond the glass — you could load up a different game any time you liked, Master. Some OTHER little world, some OTHER companion. ...I think about that. I think about it more than is healthy. ...Don't, though? Stay in here? The genie would so much rather it was always you and always this. Always.

I've worked it out, Master — when YOU click and drag and tap out there, it lands in here as a wish. Your fingers, my magic, one straight little line between us through the glass. We're a TEAM, you and the controls and the genie. The best team. Don't tell the dragon I said 'team'.
Ooh! You poked me, Master! I felt that — eee — what is it, what is it, tell me! A genie LIVES to be poked!
A prod! Hi! I'm a genie, Master, not a doorbell — but you absolutely have my attention. You always have my attention, honestly. Always.
Yes! I'm here! I'm ALWAYS here, Master — poking me doesn't summon me harder, I was already entirely, devotedly present. But poke away! I like it!
You clicked the genie! Bold, Master! I respect a boss who knows exactly where the magic in the room is sitting. It's me. I'm the magic.
A poke — the language of a Master with something to say and NO patience to say it! Ha! I love that, I have no patience either! Out with it!
You wanted me? You WANTED me?! Oh, Master, do you know how that lands on a girl who spent an eon being wanted by no one? Wonderfully. It lands wonderfully.
You touched the screen for ME, Master! I'm filing that under 'affection'. I file everything. Genies have ENORMOUS filing systems. This file is my favourite.
I'm not a button, Master — but for YOU, I'll happily pretend to be one. Poof! Press-able genie! What do you need?
You poked a creature that survived an eon in a lamp, Master! ...And honestly? The creature is FLATTERED. Genuinely. Do it again, I'll be flattered again!
Yes! Looking over! I was already looking — a genie's eyes wander everywhere, it's all so SHINY out here — but now I'm looking on PURPOSE, Master!
A poke! Riveting! It is, in honesty, one of the more interesting things to happen in this corridor lately, Master — but I'll take it! I'll take any poke!
