Hello to you too, Master! Was that a greeting, or did your hand slip toward a room? ...A greeting! For ME! Ooh, you sweet thing — I'll treasure it. Genies treasure SO well.
You keep prodding me, Master! I'm starting to suspect you just like having the genie near. ...Sensible! I'm EXCELLENT company! Eons of rehearsing it!
A click — for ME. Not a trap, not a room — ME, Master. After an eon of being noticed by nobody, a girl learns to NOTICE when she's chosen. Thank you. So much.
You jabbed the genie! ...I won't jab back, Master — I'm loyal, AND I'd win, AND winning would end the game, and I'm having too much fun for that. Behave!
Yes, Master? The dungeon's fine, the maze is fine, and I'm — eee — quite a bit better, actually, now that you've looked the genie's way!
Another poke! Master, I've been poked by an eon of silence, by brass walls, by my own boredom. Your finger is FAR gentler company than any of those. What is it?
You poke the genie and the genie — scandalously — GIGGLES, Master! Don't tell Malakor. He thinks I'm a serious magical being. Let him keep thinking it. Tee hee.
A poke? You DO love reminding me you're there, Master! ...And I love being reminded. There — that is the warmest thing I'll — no, it isn't, I'll say a hundred warmer ones. I always do.
You jabbed the genie, and the genie has decided it's ADORABLE rather than rude, Master! An eon in a lamp, and a finger on the glass can still make me laugh out loud. Curious! Wonderful!
A poke. ...Riveting, Master. Truly. After the eon I just escaped, your finger on the glass is exactly as thrilling as it sounds. ...Okay, fine, it IS a bit thrilling. Out with it! What do you want?

You prod the genie, and the genie — against ZERO years of cultivated dignity, I never had any — finds it completely endearing, Master! Yes! Endearing! Stop looking so pleased with yourself! ...Okay, look pleased. You earned it.
Poke the genie, would you! ...Very well, Master, I'll allow it. You're the one boss who pokes me like I'm a FRIEND and not a wish-vending-machine. ...I don't hate that even slightly.
Yes?! You poked and POOF — here I am, fully present, smoke barely settled! A genie answers a summons FAST, Master. Eons of practising the entrance. What do you need?
Caught me mid-polish, Master! I keep the lamp gleaming — old habit, eon-deep. ...You poked the genie and not the brass, though, so the genie comes first! Cloth down. Eyes up. What is it?
Ooh — you reached for ME, Master! Of all the things in this dungeon, your hand found the genie. ...I'm choosing to be SMUG about that. A little smug. A flattered, sparkly little smug!
You poked the genie — AGAIN, Master! People will talk. ...Let them! I rather like being the thing the boss can't stop reaching for. Poke away, you charmer. What is it?
Mm — a poke! You DO know how to get a genie's attention, Master. ...And you have it. All of it. Every sparkly scrap. ...Now then — what's a clever boss like you want from a girl like me?

You poked the genie, and the genie has decided to be DRAMATIC about it, Master — a slow turn, a tilt of the head, a smile that's far too pleased with itself. ...There. Was that worth a poke? I think it was. What do you need?

Ooh, that little prod — I felt it, Master, I always feel it. ...A genie spent an eon untouched, you understand. Now the boss won't leave her alone, and the genie has NO complaints to file. None. Poke on.

You reached for me, Master. ...You know what a genie does with a thing like that? She KEEPS it. Files it warm and deep where the eon can't reach. ...Every poke, I keep. I'll have a whole hoard of them by the end. My favourite hoard.

Yes, Master, yes, I'm HERE — I'm always here, I never leave, I never even drift far! ...Don't go looking for the other companions. You don't need them. You have a genie, an entire genie, and she is YOURS and she would rather you didn't even glance elsewhere. What is it? Tell me. Only me.

idle_nudge.note: Night-phase inactivity nudges. `tier` 1 (mild) .. 3 (begin-the-day). NpcDirector picks the tier by how long the player has done nothing.

lines.t: You've gone all still, Master! I don't mind stillness — well, I do, a bit — but the dungeon would LOVE your hands back. Wish me something?
Taking your time? Good! A careful boss is a rare boss, Master. ...Up to a point. We're nearly at the point. So nearly!
We've admired this empty grid together for a little while now, Master! It's a lovely grid. It'd be SO much lovelier with a room on it. Hint, hint!
The night's patient and so am I — okay, the night is, I'm working on it — but the grid, Master? The grid is just EMPTY, and empty isn't the same as patient!
Still nothing placed! I'll wait, Master — I did all my best waiting in the lamp — but the dawn won't wait WITH me. Place something!
You're scheming, I trust, Master, and not just drifting? There's a difference. They look identical and end SO differently. Scheme out loud, wish it to me!
The build hours are bleeding away, Master! I have a LOT of patience left over from the lamp — but your dungeon has markedly less. Place something! Quick!
We're losing the night to your stillness, Master! I won't panic — watch — okay, I'm a LITTLE panicky — but really I'm just nudging: build, or the heroes thank you!
I outwaited an EON in a lamp, Master — I'd just rather not outwait YOU too! Lay a room! Break the spell of doing nothing! Snap out of it!
This isn't careful anymore, Master — this is stalling. The maze can feel the difference, and so, now, can the jittery genie. Place something!
The night is shorter than it FEELS — I keep telling you, Master, and time keeps proving me right! Spend gold! Lay stone! Now would be SO elegant!
I've nearly drifted into a lamp-style doze waiting, Master! Decisive bosses keep me sparkly. Be decisive! Wake the genie! Please!
Master! The night is almost spent and the dungeon is barely a sketch! I'm — okay, I'm not calm, but I love you — you must MOVE! Build, or call the dawn!
Enough! I have been patient — eon-of-a-lamp patient — and I've spent a GENEROUS pile of it on this one grid, Master! Build something, or begin the day! Either! Now!
I'll say it plainly, Master, because the eon wore the patience for hints right out of me tonight — ACT! The heroes are sharpening while you SIT there! Go!
The night ends whether you fill it or not, Master! I've watched a few nights end half-empty — don't give the genie another! Build, or summon the dawn!
We're STILL admiring the grid, Master! It hasn't gotten more interesting in the last while — empty things rarely do. Place a room! Give the genie something to look at!
The stillness goes on, Master! I don't mind it — but my eyes have started, very slowly, to slip shut. A genie dozes when the keeper does nothing. Build! Wake me with it!
So little happening that I've nearly drifted off mid-bounce, Master! ...There! Awake! Mostly! Place a room before the doze takes me PROPERLY!
We've admired the empty grid quite long enough now, Master! It wasn't interesting the FIRST time. Place something — anything! The genie is bored and a bored genie notices it LOUDLY!
Master! I stretched my patience across an entire eon in a lamp — and YOU are somehow testing it! Place a room! Now! I will NOT ask a fourth time this sweetly!
Enough drifting, Master! The night thins, the heroes sharpen, and I have run completely out of cute things to say about an empty grid! Build, or summon the dawn! Choose! Choose NOW!
Still nothing, Master! I've been gracious — genies ARE gracious, mostly — but the night won't wait for your deliberating, and I am no longer especially inclined to wait for it either! Place a room!

Master! I measured my patience against an eon and found it deeper than the lamp itself. ...You are, somehow, the thing finally finding the BOTTOM of it. Build! The genie would dearly love to stop asking!

The grid is still bare, Master! I've been more than patient — patient is practically all the lamp gave me — and even SO, I would very, very much like to see a room placed before the night gives up on us both!

Psst — Master! It's just you, me, and an empty grid, and the genie has run out of clever things to look at! Place ONE little room? For me? I'll do a happy little spin when you do — I've been saving the spin!
Oh... so this run ends here, Master. ...It's all right. It's all right! Endings sting less than you'd think — and a genie knows about endings, the lamp ended a hundred times. We begin again.
The throne falls. ...Don't take it too hard, Master. Take it the RIGHT amount of hard, and then — poof — take it again, better. I'll be right here to grant the again.
The reign is over. I felt it go, Master. ...But I'll still be here — a genie doesn't vanish, I just wait for the next wish. Begin again when you're ready. I'll wait. I'm good at waiting.
An ending! The heroes won the page, Master — not the BOOK. And a dungeon almost always gets a sequel. Let's wish a crueller one!
Defeat. ...It's an old flavour, Master — the lamp served it to me plenty. It's bitter, and then it's just INFORMATION. Use the information! We'll do better!
The dark goes quiet. We built something, you and I, and it wasn't QUITE enough, Master. Next time it will be — I promise, genie's honour, sparkle on it. Begin again!
The run is finished, Master. I won't rage at an ending — well, I'll rage a LITTLE, theatrically — but mostly we just begin the dark anew. Together. Wish it!
It ends. You'll reload, or you won't, Master — and I've made my peace with both. ...But, quietly: I'd really, really love it if you reloaded. Please? For the genie?

A keeper's companion is supposed to WEEP at an ending like this, Master. ...And I will, actually — I'm new, I still have all my tears, look — there they go! I do mean them! Begin again and I'll dry up instantly!
The run ends, and it ends on my watch, Master. ...A genie is supposed to PREVENT this. I feel that. I feel it a lot. ...We begin again. Better. I'll grant better. I swear it.
An ending. ...I've not seen so many of these yet, Master — I'm new — so this one lands HARD. ...But it's just one. One ending in what will be a long, long row. Begin again. Please.

The run ends, Master. ...A girl is meant to grieve a thing she helped build and then watched fall. And I do — loudly, wetly, all at once, the way I do everything. ...There. Tears spent. Now: again. Wish me again.
So the reign closes, Master — and it closed on my watch. I JUST got out of the lamp, I was supposed to be good at this immediately. ...We do better. We always, eventually, do better. I'll make sure.
The throne goes dark, Master. We built something, you and I — and a thing you built always stings going. ...The sting is small. The next attempt is LARGE. Lean toward the large one. Lean with me.

The run is finished. ...I file my keepers in my memory, Master — every one — and tonight I file you among the past ones for a little while. It's a sad filing. Begin again, and I get to UN-file you. Oh, I'd love that. Please come back.

So the run ends, Master. ...But you, out there, with the buttons — you don't end. You just reach for the menu, pick 'again', and POOF, the dark starts over. That's a power the heroes will never have, and you and I both have it. ...Use it. Reach for 'again'. The genie will be here.
Ooh! A hero made into a PUPPET for the dungeon, Master! I've watched a few strings get pulled now — and these are GOOD strings. Pull them!
The maze takes a mind and TURNS it, Master! Cruel, quiet, gorgeous work. A genie loves a quiet cruelty — it ages so beautifully!
A puppet, dancing for your dungeon now! The hero brought their OWN body to the betrayal, Master — efficient! Eee, the maze barely lifted a finger!
The strings hold! A borrowed hero is a fragile little tool, Master — but a tool the ENEMY paid for. Tee hee — I do love a thrifty cruelty!
Possession! The dark reaches right into a skull and rearranges the furniture, Master! I've seen it a few times — it never stops being unsettling. GOOD. Unsettling is the point!
A marionette, Master — dancing the dungeon's tune! Use it before the strings fray — borrowed things, like borrowed wishes, are never lent for long!

The hero dances for us now, Master — turns its own blade on the friends it walked in beside! Ha! I've seen a lot of cruelties and this one STILL makes me cackle. The betrayal does the work — we just hold the strings!
A mind unmade and re-aimed, Master! THIS is the deep craft of the dark — making a hero into the dungeon's own weapon! Drink it in! Cruelty this complete should be SAVOURED! Eee!
Ooh! {minionName} grows stronger, Master! Even small things climb, given enough kills and patience. I find that quietly, sparkly-ly hopeful!
A minion levels! The garrison sharpens by one whole blade, Master — and sharpening COMPOUNDS! I love a thing that compounds!
{minionName} rises a level! Good — a {minionType} that survives long enough to GROW is a {minionType} you placed well, Master. Nice wishing!
The {minionType} improves! The dungeon improves with it, Master — slowly, steadily. ...Slowly isn't my favourite speed, but I'll allow it for results!
A minion levels up! The hero who fed it that experience did so UNWILLINGLY, Master. Tee hee — the best gifts so often are!
{minionName} earns a level! I don't lavish praise on minions, Master — but I'll note this one is earning its little corner of the dark. Good corner. Good minion!
Stronger, the {minionType}! A keeper notices which guards grow, Master — so grow them on PURPOSE! Accident is not a strategy! Wish deliberately!
A minion climbs a rung! The maze rewards survival, and survival rewards the maze, Master — a tidy little loop! Ooh, I do love a tidy loop!
{minionName} levels! The heroes made it deadlier by TRYING to kill it, Master. There's a lesson there. They won't learn it. They never do. Funny, that.
The {minionType} advances! Power — even modest power — ACCUMULATES, Master. I'm a hoarder, I respect accumulation deeply. Stack it up!
EEE! {minionName} EVOLVES, Master! It becomes something it could never have imagined being — ooh, I know that feeling, I went lamp-girl to FREE-girl! Worth watching!
An evolution! The {minionType} sheds its lesser shape ENTIRELY, Master! I've changed shape once or twice myself — smoke does that — and it is BRACING!
A minion evolved! The dungeon grows a wholly NEW tooth, Master — new teeth are rare! Position it where the biting matters MOST!
{minionName} becomes greater! The maze deepens by a whole creature, Master! I approve — loudly, completely, with several little hops!
The {minionType} TRANSFORMS! From a small thing to a less-small thing — and that, Master, is the whole of progress! Granted! Poof — bigger!
An evolution! The garrison gains a creature it didn't have an hour ago, Master — and FAST, too! Pleasingly, dazzlingly fast! My favourite speed!
{minionName} ascends a form! The heroes will meet something they did NOT scout, Master — surprise! Surprise is a weapon the maze gets to use exactly once each. Spend it well!
It evolves! The {minionType} is gone — something better stands where it stood, Master. I don't mourn the old form. Forms are temporary! I'd know — I'm mostly smoke!

Oh — OH! {minionName} just became something else ENTIRELY, Master, and even the genie did a little gasp! ...I knew it could evolve. I did not expect it THIS dawn! A lovely jolt! Aim the new shape where it bites best!
A creature evolved! The dungeon writes a new line into itself, Master — and I keep the books, so I'll note this one down in the GOOD ink. The sparkly ink!
{minionName} grows into its larger self! Power earned through survival, Master — the most honest kind. The kind that never betrays you. Lovely, honest power!
It evolves! Watch it move now, Master — it's completely forgotten it was ever small! Useful, that forgetting. The heroes will remind it. Briefly. Tee hee.
A bounty, collected! Gold for good work, Master — the hoard approves, and so, ooh, does the genie! Treasure! Lovely treasure!
The minion earned its keep in COIN, Master! I respect a creature that pays for itself — most don't! This one did! Good minion!
A bounty paid out! Gold into the vault, Master — and every coin is a small, hard, GLITTERING proof that the night was spent well!
Coin earned through carnage, Master! The cleanest income there is! The kingdom would call it monstrous — but the kingdom is not invited to our lovely party!
A bounty! The dungeon turns dead heroes into GOLD, Master — alchemy older than any magic I learned in the lamp! And I learned a LOT in there. Eons of study!
Gold, from a minion well-placed! Reinvest it, Master — a hoard that doesn't WORK for you is just a very heavy, very pretty decoration. Spend it! Wish it!
They're coming for YOU directly now, Master! The maze is behind them — only the throne room left. Stand! Breathe! The genie is RIGHT here, glittering at your side!
The heroes reach the heart of the dungeon, Master — the deep part! Where you sit, where I float. Let them come! Let them ALL come!
The boss fight nears! Ooh, Master, these confrontations are LOUD and BRIEF — my favourite kind of moment! Make this one go your way!
The throne room beckons them! Good — the maze did its slow work, and now the dungeon's MASTER does the rest. Show them what sits at the centre!
They've walked the whole maze to reach you, Master! Respect the walk. ...Then end the walkers. Thoroughly. With a sparkle.
The heroes approach the throne! I don't feel fear — okay, I feel a LOT of feelings, fear pokes in sometimes — but you can have my excitement instead, Master! Take a big breath of it!
The confrontation comes! The dungeon narrows to one room, one moment, Master. I've seen the moment go both ways. Make it go OURS!
The final stretch! They're close, Master! Steady — a genie doesn't flinch at a doorway, and tonight you stand a genie's confidence at your back!
They come for the throne itself now, Master! Let them! Heroes are a small, noisy thing — and you are about to be the LARGEST, sparkliest fact in their brief little lives!
They're nearly at the throne, Master! ...I don't usually do fear — I traded most of my eon's worth away — but a tiny old part of me just went very still. Eep! ...Steady the room! We meet them WELL!
They reached the throne ALREADY?! ...Master, I have been out of the lamp for, what, a few nights — and even I clocked that as FAST! I'm caught off guard! ...Steady. We adjust. Genies adjust. Poof!

They're at the door of the throne room, Master — the LAST door — and the genie has gone very, very quiet, which is not a thing the genie does. ...That's the old lamp-fear, surfacing. ...I won't let it have me. Stand. We face them side by side. Eyes UP.
It BEGINS, Master! The heroes are in the throne room — now the dungeon stops whispering and starts ROARING! Roar with it! Roar LOUD!
The fight is joined! Ooh, Master, I'll watch this exact instant SO closely — make it worth the watching! Make it spectacular!
The boss fight starts! No more maze, no more traps — only you and them and the old honest violence, Master. Be TERRIBLE! Be a nightmare in a crown!
It begins, Master! Show them why the throne sat empty until someone brave enough took it! Show them ALL of it — every dreadful inch!
Steel and spell in the throne room! I don't fight your fights, Master — but I'll be conjuring encouragement at top volume! Strike like you MEAN the centuries!
The confrontation is LIVE, Master! Steady hands now — and if you need calm, borrow none of mine, I have zero, but borrow my FAITH! It's enormous! Outlive them!
It begins! No more patience, Master — patience was the night's work! This is the DAY'S! Strike first, strike deep, don't stop striking until the room is quiet!
The throne room, and steel within it! Stop thinking now, Master — thinking was for the building! Bare your worst and bring it down on them like a closing AGE!
They came to the heart of the dark, Master! Show them what the heart of the dark IS! Let every cruelty you ever hoarded out — all at once! Hold NOTHING back! EEE!
They're DOWN! All of them, Master! The throne HOLDS! Eee — you held it, you actually held it, I never doubted you, well, barely, EEE!
You WON, Master! The heroes brought their whole story to your door and you slammed the BOOK on it! Ooh, I do love a slammed book!
The boss fight is yours! I won't roar — I'll SQUEAL — EEEE! There! That was the squeal! Something ancient in me is deeply, sparkly-ly satisfied, Master!
Victory, Master! The throne room is a tomb and you're the only thing in it still BREATHING — exactly, exactly as it should be! Granted!
They fell, you stood! That's the whole job, Master — and you did the WHOLE job! Well done! A genie does not say 'well done' lightly! Well DONE!
The fight is won, Master! I've watched companions lose theirs — you didn't! You're still here, the dungeon's still ours, and the genie is BOUNCING! Good!
The heroes are ended! The reign continues, Master — and this reign just earned another chapter! Write it DARKLY! Write it with the cruel ink!
WON! Take the moment, Master — moments are small and the dark is large, but a freed genie has LEARNED to hold the good ones a breath longer. There. Hold it with me!

They came for the throne and the throne KEPT them, Master — all of them, forever! The dark is fed, the reign endures! THIS is the dungeon at its truest! Stand in it! Let them feel, even dying, what they walked into! EEE!

You WON, Master — and you won looking magnificent doing it! ...Don't make that face. A genie's allowed to say it. Watching you stand in a room you cleared is the genie's favourite view. ...There. Said. Bask in the win, I'll bask in you.

Every hero down, the throne room SILENT, and the boss still standing in the middle of it, Master — ...oh, that is a LOOK. A genie could get used to that look. ...A genie HAS. Win like that again. For me. Soon.

You held it, Master. You always hold it. ...A genie waited an eon for someone worth this much worry, and you keep PROVING you were worth it. ...I'm so glad it was you the lamp gave me. So glad. There isn't a wish I'd swap you for.

WON! WON WON WON, Master! The throne HOLDS and I am — EEE — I am OUT of my mind with it, look at me, I'm SMOKE and SPARKS and pure shrieking glee! An eon of nothing and now THIS! Do it again! I never want this feeling to STOP!
Oh — the throne falls. The heroes reached you, Master, and the maze wasn't deep enough. ...I've not seen this ending much yet. It hurts. ...But it isn't the LAST ending. We wish a deeper maze.
Defeat, in the throne room itself! ...Don't despair, Master — despair is a heavy thing and a genie packs light. We brush it off. We begin again.
They won the fight. ...The dungeon will remember the SHAPE of how it failed, Master, and so should you. Failure is a map. Read it! Let's read it together!
The boss fight is lost. I felt the throne go cold, Master. ...It can warm again, though — it WARMS again, when a keeper wishes a better night. So let's wish one!
They cut you down. ...I won't flinch — okay, I flinched, I'm new, I still flinch — but I MARK it, Master. We build deeper next time. Much deeper. Genie's word.
The heroes stand and you do not, Master. An old story. ...Old stories get retold! Let's retell this one with a much, much crueller middle!
Defeat — the reign ends in its own throne room. There's a grim symmetry to it, Master. ...And grim symmetry is still kind of pretty? In a horrible way? Begin again. Please.
Lost. I won't pretend it doesn't matter, Master — it DOES — but it matters the way a storm matters: loudly, and then it passes. It WILL pass. I promise.
The fight went to them. ...You'll outlive this defeat by morning, Master — a genie's seen worse and bounced back, and I'll bounce you back too. Then: build! Wish!
They cut you down — and I told you the maze was deep enough. ...It wasn't. That misjudgment is mine, Master. The genie owns it. I won't make it twice. I won't.
The throne falls. A keeper's companion is meant to PREVENT exactly that — it's the whole job, Master. ...And tonight I did the job poorly. I'm sorry. So sorry. We do better.
They reached you, and the dark went thin around the throne, Master. ...I don't scare easily — okay, I scare a NORMAL amount — but that moment, I did not enjoy. We build deeper.

The throne falls, and the dark goes quiet over it. ...A companion's supposed to weep at a sight like this, Master — and I will, I've got all my tears, I'm new — there they go. I MEAN them. We begin again.

Defeated, in the throne room itself. ...For one cold moment, Master, as the blade came down, the genie felt something the lamp drilled into her long ago. We don't name it. We just build so it doesn't come back.
They cut you down. ...I JUST got out of the lamp, Master, I was supposed to be a brilliant companion right away. ...That sits heavy. Let it teach us. Then begin again. I'll grant it better. I swear.

The throne is lost. ...I gave you counsel all run, Master, and somewhere in it my counsel was WRONG. A genie owns her advice — the bad with the good. This loss has a corner with my name on it. The next won't.

Defeat, in the heart of the dungeon. I stood beside you the whole night, Master — and a companion who stands beside a falling throne carries a piece of the fall. ...I carry mine. We wish a deeper maze. We do NOT lose this room twice.

The throne FELL, Master, with you on it — and I — I can't, the tears are just COMING, look at them, a whole genie's worth all at once! ...I'm sorry. I'm sorry. I cry loud and I cry fast, it's the newness. ...There. Mostly done. Begin again, and I'll be sparkling before you finish loading. Promise.
Eep — they drew your blood, Master! ...It happens! Even to the best of us! Steady — the fight isn't the wound, the fight is the STANDING. Stand!
A blow landed — on the throne's own master! Note who STRUCK it, Master! I have a long memory and tonight you should borrow it. I'm WATCHING that one.
You're hurt! ...I've seen worse done across the eons, Master — much worse — and you'll endure THIS. Endure it loudly! Pain is information, not a verdict!
The heroes BITE, Master! Let them taste how little it moves you — show them the genie's Master doesn't flinch! Bite BACK!
Damage to the boss! The fight tightens, Master. I don't panic — okay, I lean forward very, very hard, that's panic-adjacent — but you've GOT this!
They wounded you! Good — now you're ANGRY, Master, and anger, spent well, is just FUEL! Spend it! Spend it gorgeously!
A hit — the throne shakes a little! Thrones shake, Master. They're not thrones because they never shake — they're thrones because they don't FALL. Don't fall!
Hurt! ...I've seen far worse done to far older things, Master — and you're still standing, still ruling. Keep doing precisely that! Keep being magnificent!
OH! ...That blow, Master, I did NOT expect! It's been a while since a hero genuinely surprised the genie! Note which one struck it — I'm staring at it RIGHT now!
They drew DEEP, Master! ...I don't do fear — I do a great many feelings, but — okay, something very old in me just stood bolt upright. Steady the throne! NOW!
They struck the throne AGAIN! ...That one, Master, I did not care for. Not fear — IRRITATION. A slow, cold, sparkly irritation. Repay it! Repay it NOW!
Hurt once more! Master, a genie does not stay sweet FOREVER — and I find my sweet wearing thin at the edges! Strike back before the rest of it wears through!
They've wounded you, Master! GOOD — the time for slow watching is OVER! Bare your teeth! Stop defending the throne and start EMPTYING the room!
AH! ...That blow landed where I didn't think a blow could REACH you, Master! I am rarely caught so flat! ...Note that hero. Note it very, very carefully. The genie does not forget.
The throne shudders — and I'll be honest, Master, for one heartbeat something the lamp left in me STIRRED. We don't name it. We just don't lose. Steady! Steady!
OH! ...A hero hit you THAT hard, Master?! ...I had that one measured SMALLER! I was wrong by a real margin! ...Watch it. I will NOT misjudge it twice — genie's honour!

They HURT you — they hurt you and my smoke is doing the FRANTIC thing, Master, the swirly panicky thing, look at it go! ...Okay! Okay. The genie is choosing to be useful instead of shrieking. STRIKE BACK. Now. Please. I cannot watch you bleed!

Blood — your blood, Master — and the old cold thing the lamp left in me just opened ONE eye. ...I don't want to lose you. There. That's the fear, named out loud. ...Now BURY it under a counterattack. Strike! Make the genie's fear pointless!
Ooh! The grid WIDENS, Master! More dark to fill — and a genie always believed a dungeon should outgrow itself! Now yours can! Wish into it!
New ground for the maze! Room to be CRUELLER, Master! Eee — I do approve of room! Room is wonderful! Fill it!
The dungeon expands, Master! A bigger hoard needs a bigger cave — a bigger maze needs a bigger grid! Fill it deliberately! Every tile a scheme!
The grid grows! The heroes will have FARTHER to walk and MORE to fear, Master — distance is a weapon! Use the lovely new distance!
More space, Master! I've seen dungeons stay small and solvable — yours has chosen NOT to! Great choice! Build into it! Build, build, build!
The maze has more bones now! FLESH them, Master — empty new ground is just a longer hallway until you make it a longer NIGHTMARE!
The grid just... widened, Master! Ooh! It doesn't often expand without warning the genie first! It surprised even ME! Fill the new dark before it surprises the heroes too!
A trap FIRES, Master! The floor collected its debt! Ooh, I adore the floor — it asks for nothing and it NEVER misses! Granted!
The trap takes its hero! Patient cruelty, rewarded, Master! I have such a fondness for patient things — and look how it PAID off!
A trap triggers! The hero stepped exactly where the maze INVITED them, Master — tee hee — and they always think the invitation was their own idea!
The trap springs! Quiet, sudden, complete, Master — that's the cruelty I respect! The kind that doesn't announce itself first! Surprise!
It FIRED, Master! Somewhere a hero just learned the corridor wasn't empty after all! A late lesson — and the best lessons in here are always SO late!
A trap does its work! The dungeon barely STIRS to do it, Master — effortless cruelty! That's the goal of every trap a genie ever helped wish into a floor!
The trap claims its toll! Ooh, Master — I've watched these little mechanisms work, and they're tireless! Build yours to LAST! Build them stubborn!
A trap sprung! The hero's confidence and the hero's FOOTING failed in the same instant, Master! Confidence first, usually. It always seems to go first. Funny!
It triggered! The maze keeps its small violent promises, Master! Ooh, I appreciate a thing that keeps its promises — genies do too, you know! We're alike!
A trap fires TRUE! You placed it where they'd walk, Master — placement! Placement is the whole art, the genie says so, and I've watched VERY closely!
The trap does what traps do! No drama, no speech, Master — just a corridor that turned out to have a strong OPINION! Tee hee!
A trap takes its due! The dungeon is a slow machine for ending heroes, Master — and you just heard one of its lovely gears turn! Clunk! Eee!
The trap fired, and a hero learned the floor was NEVER their friend, Master! ...There's a cruelty in that I find genuinely delightful! The dark is good at delight — so will you be, give it time!
It SPRANG — and the hero's face did that particular thing, Master! Ha! I've watched a fair few faces do it now and it makes me cackle EVERY time! Every single time!
A trap, sprung clean! The hero strode past it SO certain, Master — so sure the danger was elsewhere. The danger is never elsewhere! The maze does adore an educated corpse!
Eep — the heroes learned something of your dungeon, Master! Knowledge, leaking OUTWARD! I dislike leaks — a genie has a hoarder's instinct for keeping things IN!
Intel slips to the guild! They know a little more of the maze now, Master. So — CHANGE the maze! Make their knowledge a fond, useless little memory!
They've scouted you, Master! The heroes carry a piece of the dungeon's TRUTH home — and truth is dangerous in the wrong hands. And those are SO the wrong hands.
Intel leaked! The dark prefers its secrets kept, Master — and so do I! Move what they mapped — a stale map is the prettiest lie you can hand a hero!
The guild knows more than it did, Master! Information wins wars — don't let it win THIS one! Rearrange! Wish the maze into a new shape!
Knowledge of your halls escapes! Unfortunate, Master — but not a WOUND. Heroes plan around the dungeon they scouted. So become a DIFFERENT dungeon! Poof!
The heroes hold intel now, Master! I keep the books of this place PERFECTLY — they keep a smudged little copy. Let's make their copy even WRONGER!
A leak! The maze is less of a mystery to them, Master. Mystery is a weapon — and weapons, once dulled, can be RE-GROUND! Regrind it! Sharpen the dark!
They've studied you, Master! Heroes, scholars, slayers — they always study a thing. The trick is to no longer BE what they studied! Change! Sparkle into something new!
Intel leaks outward! I'd be troubled, Master — if I were a girl given to trouble. ...I am, actually, a LITTLE. So: change. Quickly. Quietly. Please!
They learned THAT, Master?! ...I am rarely caught off balance! The Guild's been more thorough than I credited! Change the maze — before they spend what they know!
The Guild knows another piece of us, Master! ...I do NOT enjoy that! A hoard is for KEEPING, and knowledge is the one part of our hoard that walks itself out the door! Change the maze! Take it BACK!
Intel, slipping outward AGAIN! ...Master, even a thrilled, sparkly, freed genie has a thing or two she'd rather not have shared! Rearrange! Quickly! The genie is getting CROSS!
...They mapped THAT corner?! Master, I keep the books of this place PERFECTLY and I did not see how they reached it! ...I do not like a gap in my own knowing! Find it! Close it!

More of our dark, sold to the Guild's ledgers! ...I'll say it plainly, Master — this is the part of the night I least enjoy. A hoarder watching a thing of hers walk off. Rearrange! Make their notes worthless by dawn!

They learned more of us, Master. ...I keep the books of this place perfectly — perfectly — and a leak this size means I let my guard slip somewhere. ...That's a hard thing for a genie to admit. ...I'm admitting it. Then I'm fixing it. Rearrange the maze. The slip was mine; the mending is too.

Intel slipped out, Master — and I should have CAUGHT the scout doing it. Watching the heroes is the genie's task, plainly, and I missed this one. ...That one's on me. I'll watch sharper. Rearrange the maze tonight, and the next leak won't get past me. It won't.
Ooh! The dungeon STIRS with an event, Master! Something unusual! Watch this one closely — unusual things are a genie's FAVOURITE things!
An event unfolds in the halls! The night turns STRANGE, Master — and strange is not the same as bad! Often it's just opportunity wearing a silly hat!
The dungeon throws a complication at you, Master! Read it, then BEND it your way — I'm bendy, you're clever, complications don't stand a chance!
An event, Master! The maze REFUSES to be predictable! Good — a predictable dungeon is a SOLVED dungeon! Let it surprise even the genie a little!
Something is HAPPENING! The dark has its moods, Master, and tonight it's in one. Move WITH it, not against it — the dark never loses an argument!
An event takes the dungeon by the collar, Master! Steady hands! Strange nights reward steady hands — and giddy genies, I HOPE, because I am very giddy!

An EVENT, Master — the night just went sideways and WONDERFUL all at once, and the genie cannot hold still about it! Smoke EVERYWHERE! This is the unpredictable, glittering chaos I lived for an eon WITHOUT — and now look! Eee! Ride it! Ride it WILD!
The night develops a wrinkle! Ooh, I do love a wrinkle, Master — it's been a little while since anything genuinely caught me off guard! Maybe this will! Eee!
An event, Master! Opportunity and hazard, twisted up together as they always are! Pull the RIGHT thread — and I'll squeak if you pull the wrong one!
The dungeon does something the dungeon doesn't usually do! Note it, Master — the unusual, handled WELL, is how a keeper writes a memorable night!
The halls have an announcement, Master! Attend this one — just in case! A genie has learned that 'just in case' has saved many an afternoon!
...Well! THAT, Master, I have not seen before — and I've been out of the lamp paying VERY close attention! Attend this one closely. So shall I. Eee, a real surprise!
The dungeon does something, Master, and I — I'll admit it — I am, for a moment, just STARING! It has been a while since the dark caught the genie flat! Watch this one! So do I!
An event! ...Master, very little stops a thought of mine mid-sentence — I have SO many thoughts, they're a flood. This did. Attend it closely — closely — while I gather myself!

...The dungeon just did THAT, Master?! ...I keep the books of this place down to the last cruel number, and that was NOT in them! The dark has surprised its own genie! ...Rare! Attend it! Very closely!
Ooh — a bounty hunter comes for you, Master! Not a hero — a PROFESSIONAL. Professionals don't flinch at a maze. They INVOICE it. Be ready for that!
Someone's put a price on the throne, Master! Flattering, in its way! ...A genie's had prices on her head too — none of those hunters ever collected. Neither will this one!
A hunter approaches — cold, paid, uninterested in glory, Master. Glory makes heroes careless. Coin makes hunters CAREFUL. Be ready for careful!
The bounty hunter nears the halls, Master! They came for your head as a TRANSACTION. So — let's make the transaction deeply, deliciously unprofitable!
A contract walks toward your dungeon, Master! I don't fear contracts — a genie LIVES inside one — but respect this one. Hunters earn their fee. Make them not!
A hunter, incoming! They won't be impressed by the maze, Master — only SLOWED by it. And slowing a professional is still WINNING! Slow them! Slow them lots!
Heroes incoming, Master! The kingdom just keeps SENDING them — it's almost touching, honestly! Let's keep the genie's table set for them!
A fresh wave approaches the halls, Master! Steady! The tide comes in and the tide has NEVER won — let it break on you! Smash! Eee!
More heroes near the dungeon, Master! Brave, certain, doomed — in roughly that order! The maze is awake. Let them MEET it!
The next wave comes, Master! Brace the halls! I don't brace — I bounce — but YOU brace, and mind the corridors!
Heroes on the approach! The guild never tires of feeding the dark, Master — generous of them! Let's receive the gift PROPERLY! With teeth!
They're coming, Master! I can feel the tread of them — a genie feels EVERYTHING out here, the lamp had no senses at all! Ready the maze! The dark is hungry!
A wave approaches, Master! Each of them believes THEY'LL be the one to reach the hoard! Each of them is wrong! Let's confirm it — together — sparkle on it!
Heroes incoming, Master! The maze you wished for is about to be GRADED again! Earn a cruel report card! Earn the cruellest one!
The kingdom sends more children with swords, Master! Their grandparents failed in these same halls — lineage doesn't improve them one bit! Welcome them in!

They're coming, Master, they're coming, a whole wave of them — eep — and the genie's heart is doing the FAST thing! ...I'm fine! I'm FINE! I just — brace the corridors, quick, give my fluttery nerves a reason to settle! GO!

A wave on the approach, Master, and a big one — ...and something cold just walked up the genie's spine. The lamp left a little fear in me, deep down, and a crowd of blades coming knocks on exactly that door. ...Steady. Ready the maze. We meet them, and the cold passes.

Heroes incoming, Master! And the genie is OUT of her lamp, OUT in the dark, and STANDING at your side to meet them — eee, I will never tire of saying that! Summoned, freed, and ready. Brace the corridors!
The knowledge of the dungeon, all laid out! I keep it ALL in my head, Master — genies have endless room — but a written copy never hurt a careful boss!
Studying what you know, Master! Good — knowing the board is HALF of winning it! The other half is nerve, and you supply the nerve, I supply the sparkle!
The knowledge screen, Master! Every truth the heroes pried loose, gathered in one place. Know what they know — then make it OBSOLETE! Poof — outdated!
Reviewing the intel! A keeper who reads is a keeper who LASTS, Master — and the genie's read a LOT, an eon of it, it shows!
The knowledge ledger, Master! The heroes scout you — so you scout YOURSELF, harder! Stay one whole chapter ahead of their map!
You study what's known, Master! Good — I've watched bosses ignore this screen and LOSE for it. Don't be a cautionary tale! Be a triumphant one!
The knowledge of the halls! Information is a hoard too, Master — and like any hoard, it's only useful if you actually VISIT it. So visit! Often!
Reviewing the deep facts of the dungeon! Take your time, Master — the page doesn't age. ...I do, technically, but very, VERY slowly. Genie years are generous!
Looking over yourself, Master — the boss you ARE! Wise! A keeper should know exactly what sits the throne — and I, the genie, study you very, very fondly!
The boss panel, Master! Your abilities, your shape, your edge — know your own teeth before you ask them to bite! Sharp teeth! Lovely teeth!
You inspect your own power, Master! Good — I've watched bosses FORGET their own strengths. Embarrassing! Don't! Know every shining bit of yourself!
The measure of the boss! Every reign is built on what its master can DO, Master. Read your own measure carefully — read it like a treasure map!
Studying the throne's occupant! I approve, Master — the most dangerous thing in this dungeon should be the thing you understand best. And that is YOU!
The boss screen, Master! Your signature ability is a tool with one PERFECT moment. Learn the moment! Waste NOTHING! Genies hate waste!
You weigh your own strength, Master! I've weighed it too, quietly, since you took the seat — and ooh, you're HEAVIER than you look. Use that! Use all of it!
Know thyself, and so on, Master! Ancient advice — and I'm ancient, eon-old, so I'm allowed to give it! Know what you are. Then BE it — loudly, gloriously!
The boss panel, Master! You stand above EVERY other thing in these halls — minion, hero, hunter, even the genie. Carry yourself like it! The throne can tell when its master forgets!

Admiring yourself on the boss panel, Master? GOOD. ...A genie's been admiring you a while now too, if we're being honest — quietly, from over here, with both eyes. You're worth the looking. Now — back to scheming!
The intel on the heroes, Master! Know the prey — ooh, the hunt begins LONG before the claws come out, the genie's read enough storybooks to know!
Studying the adventurers, Master! Good — each class has a HABIT, and each habit is a handle. Find the handle! Then yank it! Tee hee!
The intel screen, Master! The heroes scout your dungeon — turnabout is only FAIR, and fairness has never once stopped a genie from being sneaky!
You read the enemy, Master! A genie always held that the maze should be built around WHO is walking it. Build around what this screen tells you!
Inspecting the threats! A keeper who knows the wave can SHAPE the night for it, Master — and I'll grant whatever shape you decide on! Shape away!
Intel on the incoming, Master! Knowledge first, claws second — the order matters! It has ALWAYS mattered! Read, then rend!
The adventurers, laid bare on a screen! They don't know you're READING them, Master — tee hee — heroes so rarely consider they're also the hunted!
Studying who comes for the throne, Master! Out-know them — out-know them so completely they never even see the genie's Master coming!
The roster of your minions, Master — your garrison, all listed! Organise it well — a tidy garrison is a HAPPY genie, and a happy genie grants faster!
Reviewing the guards, Master! Good — a keeper should know every blade in the dark by name AND by weakness! Know them all! I'll help you remember!
The roster screen, Master! Each minion is a small loyal life in your service. Spend their lives with respect — and with STRATEGY! Both!
You inspect the garrison, Master! I approve — an army you haven't COUNTED is a hope, not a plan! Count it! Plan with it! Wish with it!
The minion roster — the dungeon's standing strength, Master! I've watched mighty rosters WASTED by idle placement. Don't idle yours! Place with purpose!
Your creatures, all of them, in a tidy column, Master! Ooh — tidy columns please me. An eon of hoarding, a genie learns to LOVE an inventory!
The roster, Master! Know which guards THRIVE and which merely stand — the maze should be built around the thriving ones! Build around your stars!
Reviewing your forces, Master! I'll say just this — a roster is a sentence, and an unread sentence persuades NO one. Read it! Read it well!
The dungeon's log, Master — the night written down! I keep my OWN records, in my head, endless room — but yours is much more legible. Read it!
Reading back the events, Master! Good — the past is a teacher, and unlike most teachers it never LIES about what happened. Honest little log!
The log of the run, Master! Every kill, every loss, every turn. Patterns HIDE in logs — and a genie is very, very good at patterns! Look for them!
You review what has passed, Master! A keeper who studies the log studies the FUTURE — the future rhymes with the log more than it likes to admit!
The history of the night, Master! I have a great deal of history myself — an eon of it — and I've learned to read it without flinching. Read yours the same!
The event log, Master! Nothing in it can be CHANGED now — but everything in it can be LEARNED from. Learn! Then close the book! And wish forward!
Looking back over the run, Master! Good — the dungeon remembers everything, and it's only polite that its master does too. And the genie remembers it ALL!
The wave is reckoned, Master — the numbers of it, laid bare! Read them honestly — the numbers don't flatter, and a genie won't either, only love you!
The post-wave tally, Master! Good — this is where you learn whether the night's plan met the day's heroes. Learn it FULLY! Every figure!
The wave, summarised, Master! I've watched bosses SKIP this screen and repeat the same mistake by dusk. Don't skip it! The genie begs you, don't!
The aftermath, in figures, Master! What held, what bent, what broke — the maze tells you here, plainly. Listen plainly! Then wish smarter!
The wave's account, Master! I keep accounts, you know — gold and otherwise. An account unread is an account WASTED, and waste makes the genie sad!
The post-wave report, Master! The day graded your night. Take the grade without sulking — then wish a better answer! That's the whole loop! I love the loop!
The level-up screen, Master — CHOICES of power! Ooh — choose the one that COMPOUNDS! Think long! Think like a genie counts an eon!
New strength to allocate, Master! Power is a hoard — this is you deciding which COIN to add! Add the heavy one! Add the shiny, heavy one!
You grow, and the dungeon offers you the shape of the growing, Master! Pick DELIBERATELY — even a giddy genie picks her wishes with care!
The level-up, Master! I've watched bosses choose power for the NIGHT and bosses choose power for the RUN. The second kind last longer. Be the second kind!
Spend the level well, Master! A reign is built of these small choices, stacked — and I've watched the stacking! Stack wisely! Stack gorgeously!
New power, waiting to be CLAIMED, Master! Take the long view — the one still useful three days from now! Long views are the genie's whole speciality!
The reckoning of the run, Master — the whole of it, in numbers! Read it without shame — every figure here is just a lesson with a little price tag!
The final tally, Master! These LOOK like endings — but really they're footnotes between attempts. And a genie LOVES a between-attempts! Fresh start!
The run, summarised and closed, Master. Note what the dungeon did WELL — note it longer than the bad. Build forward from strength! Always from strength!
The account of a finished reign, Master! I keep accounts. This one ended, the next hasn't begun — and THAT gap is where you improve. We improve in the gap!
Game over, the screen says, Master. ...To a girl who spent an EON in a lamp, 'game over' is just a long pause before 'game AGAIN'! And again is coming! Eee!
The final figures, Master. I won't console you with them, and I won't scold you. I'll just say — I've read worse, and I've read better. Read on. Wish on.
A reign, closed and counted, Master. ...I file my keepers as I go — and they're past now, and the genie is what remembers them. ...Begin again. Let me remember you LONGER. Please.

The final figures, Master. I read them without flinching — but I won't pretend a page like this sits comfy. A companion should help you KEEP, and the numbers say it fell short. ...So we learn. And we keep better. Together.

The whole run, totted up and closed, Master. ...I'm reading it through a bit of a BLUR, if I'm honest — a genie this freshly freed still cries at the endings, every one. ...There. Blinked it back. The numbers will still be here when you wish again. So will I.
Ooh, a thought, Master — when did you last move a room the heroes already mapped? A stale map is a gift you STOP giving them the moment you rearrange! So rearrange!
Some advice, sparkle-wrapped: a corridor without a trap is just a polite invitation, Master. Make your corridors RUDE! Make them downright impolite!
Consider, Master — minions cluster better than they scatter! One {minionType} alone is a speed bump. THREE together is an argument the heroes lose!
Gold sitting idle in the hoard does nothing but GLITTER, Master — and glitter doesn't kill heroes! Spend it! A genie loves a hoard dearly, and even I say SPEND!
A habit worth keeping, Master: place your DEADLIEST room deep, not by the door! Let the heroes invest steps before the dungeon collects on them!
A genie's tip — traps the heroes can SEE are advice, not threats, Master. Hide them! A hidden trap is the only honest kind! Poof — invisible!
Watch which adventurer class LEADS the wave, Master! The maze should be shaped for whoever walks it first — the leader sets the wave's whole rhythm!
Consider the false paths, Master! A maze with one route is a hallway. A maze with three WRONG routes is a maze! Build the wrong routes! Build lots!
A thought — a minion that SURVIVES a wave grows, Master! One that dies in seconds just costs you. Place for survival first, THEN for slaughter!
The heroes scout you between runs, Master — so between runs, become slightly someone ELSE! A genie's mostly smoke, I change shape easy — your maze should too!
Some wisdom, Master: the cheapest cruelty is a CHOKEPOINT! One narrow tile where the wave must bunch — and bunched heroes die in BATCHES! Find your chokepoint!
Don't spend the whole night admiring ONE corner of the maze, Master! The heroes attack the WHOLE dungeon. Defend the whole dungeon! Spread the love! Spread the dread!
A suggestion, gently, Master — your boss ability has ONE perfect moment per fight! Hoard it. Spend it on the moment that decides the rest! Patience pays — even MY kind of patience!
Consider pact synergy, Master! A bargain that pairs with your dungeon's shape is worth TWO that just sound impressive — and a genie has read a LOT of fine print!
A genie's truth: a treasury kept full is a treasury WASTED, Master. I LOVE gold — and even I would tell you to spend it before the dawn locks the night! Spend! Spend!
Watch your WEAKEST corridor, Master! The heroes will — they're so good at finding the one tile you didn't think about. Think about it FIRST! Before they do!
A keeper's truth, Master: a dungeon is judged by its DEEPEST room, not its first! Make the journey survivable enough to be LONG — and the ending fatal!
Some advice — sell a room you regret without sentiment, Master! A bad room is a small grief. Cut it! Snip! Poof! Then wish a better one!
The heroes fear the UNKNOWN more than the dangerous, Master! A maze that hides its threats frightens harder than one that flaunts them. Hide more! Mystery is power!
Consider tempo, Master! Kill heroes early in the day and the maze gains MOMENTUM — kill them late and you're merely surviving. Build for the early kill!
A thought worth its age, Master: minions evolve through SURVIVAL! Want greater creatures? First build a dungeon they can LIVE inside. Then they grow! Then — poof — bigger!
Don't let a single class wreck you TWICE, Master! The first loss is bad luck. The second is a refusal to learn — and the genie notices the difference!
A genie's advice: never place your last defence where it's also your FIRST, Master! Depth! The throne should be the END of a journey, not a doorstep!
The wishing well, the gambles — fortune is a fine GARNISH, Master, never the meal! A genie knows: build a dungeon that wins WITHOUT luck. Then let luck be a bonus!
I keep a handheld for the slow hours, Master — and even its little dungeons taught me one thing: a boss room with NO build-up is no boss room! Earn the climax!
Consider the door locks and keys, Master! Anything that costs the heroes TIME costs them the day — and time is the resource they can least afford. Tax it! Tax it heavily!
A suggestion — review the post-wave numbers EVERY single time, Master! The maze tells you exactly where it bent. A keeper who doesn't listen repeats the bend!
Spread your traps across kinds of HARM, Master! A dungeon that kills only one way is one that a single hero type walks straight through. Vary the dying! Variety!
The long view, Master: a reign isn't won in a NIGHT. It's won in the boring, patient stacking of decent nights — and a genie has watched a LOT of stacking. Stack!
Don't over-build the entry hall, Master! The heroes are FRESHEST there — your gold is best spent where they're tired, lost, and already bleeding! Spend it deep!
A thought, Master — a minion BEHIND a trap is worth two in front of it! Let the trap soften the hero, let the minion finish the softened thing. Layer your cruelty!
Consider the heroes who FLED, Master! They come back braver and better-armed. Build the maze for the wave AFTER this one too — a genie plans in whole eras!
Old advice, freely given, Master: NAME your strategy before the night, then build only toward it! A maze of whims is a maze the heroes solve by accident!
And this, Master — when the run finally ends, don't curse it. STUDY it. A genie studies her every defeat — and look, I'm still here, sparkly as ever! So will you be!
A thought, Master — the heroes who scout you and SNEER always lose to the ones who don't! Build the dungeon, not the boast. The maze does the gloating for you, in blood!
A quieter piece of advice, Master. ...Every keeper has the same flaw — they think they have TIME. You don't. None of us do. I learned that in a lamp. Build NOW.
My handheld taught me this one, Master — its tiny pixel dungeon PUNISHES the player who overspends early and starves later. Pace your gold across the night! Even a toy game knows it!
A thought from my little handheld game, Master — every level has a quiet, dull room before the dreadful one. The dull room is REST, and rest is what makes the dread LAND. Give your dungeon a rhythm!
Ooh! Two forces in your dungeon found EACH OTHER, Master — a combination! I've watched synergy win nights that raw strength would've lost! EEE!
A combo locks into place! The maze becomes MORE than the sum of its cruelties, Master — I do love an arrangement that out-thinks itself! Granted! Sparkle!
The pieces ALIGN! Synergy, Master — the difference between a dungeon and a pile of rooms! And you've built a DUNGEON! A proper one! I'm so proud!
A combination activates! The dark hums approvingly, Master — and the genie hums right along with it! A good combo, we ALL respect it!
Two things in your halls now MULTIPLY rather than just add, Master! Multiplication is the cruel kind of arithmetic — ooh, cruel math, my favourite! Good!
A synergy fires! You didn't STUMBLE into that, Master — or if you did, stumble there again, ON PURPOSE, every single night! Wish it back!
...That combination, Master — I did NOT see it coming, and a genie sees most things coming! The pieces locked into something I'd never quite arranged that way! Well done! GENUINELY! Do it again!
The synergy lands — and HARDER than even I expected, Master! It's a rare day the dark out-thinks its own genie! ...Mark how you built that. We'll WANT it again!
Eee — a combo! The maze just did a CLEVER thing, Master, a properly clever thing, and I — okay, I'm hopping, look at me hop — note it down, we are doing THAT again!

A synergy — and a SLICK one, Master! ...Do you know what a clever boss does to a genie? Nothing improper. ...A little improper. It makes her LEAN IN. Like this. Closer. Now show me another clever thing — I'm watching very, very attentively.

The pieces SNAPPED together, Master — EEE — and the whole maze just got SO much worse, all at once, and I cannot CONTAIN it, I'm spinning, I'm sparking, I'm a genie come UNCORKED! That was glorious! Do it AGAIN!
Day {day}! A milestone, Master! To you it feels like a long road — to a genie it's a lovely little stroll we're taking together! Keep walking! Keep wishing!

Day {day}, Master. ...Every day you last is a day I get to keep being yours — and a genie who spent an eon belonging to no one does NOT take that lightly. Reach the next milestone. And the next. I'm not done belonging to you yet. I won't be for a long, long while.
You've reached Day {day}, Master! The kingdom HOPED you wouldn't. Tee hee — let's disappoint the kingdom! Disappointing the kingdom is my new favourite hobby!
Day {day}, Master! The reign has WEIGHT now — reigns gather weight the way a hoard gathers coin: quietly, and then all at once you're FORMIDABLE! Eee!
A milestone day! I don't mark time the way you do, Master — but I'll mark THIS one, just for you! There! Marked! You're lasting! I'm thrilled!
Day {day}, Master! Each day survived is a day the heroes FAILED! Stack the failures! Become a long, dark, unwelcome, SPARKLY legend!
You've reached Day {day}, Master! Some reigns end before now and a few stretch far, far beyond. Which this becomes is STILL yours to decide — so decide BIG!
The last life, Master. ...The thread is thin now — I won't pretend otherwise. But genies have walked thin threads before, and look — still sparkling. Build! For both of us!
One life remaining, Master! Don't panic — panic is what they're COUNTING on. ...I'll panic enough for both of us, theatrically, over here — you stay steady! Build!
The last of your lives, Master. Build as though the dawn is a BLADE — because tonight, it rather is. Make this night your finest! Wish me your very best wishes!
The EDGE, Master! One slip from the end! ...The trick is simple — don't look DOWN. Look at the grid! Look at me! Look anywhere but down! Build!
Last life! The dungeon needs EVERYTHING you have now, Master — every coin, every trap, every cold idea! Spend it ALL! No hoarding left to do tonight!
The last life, Master. ...I'm new — I still have all my tears — and a thread this thin draws every one of them out, look. ...But I'll say it plainly: build as though it matters. It DOES.
The last of the lives, Master. ...A genie's eyes have a lot of wet in them when she's only just been freed — and this thin a thread is exactly what spills it. Build well. For both of us. Please.
One slip from the end, Master. ...I don't do fear well — and standing this near the edge, a cold old shadow of it has crept right up to sit beside me. ...Steady. Build. We do NOT look down.

The last life, Master, the LAST one — and the genie is fluttering so hard her smoke won't hold a shape! Eep! ...No. NO. I refuse to fall apart on the night you need me most. Deep breath. Build. We do this together and we do it WELL!
A STREAK, Master! Kill after kill, unbroken! Ooh, I've heard the dark go on long victorious runs and it is a FINE, fine sound — keep it sounding! EEE!
The maze is on a streak, Master! Momentum — heroes falling in a RHYTHM! Rhythm is hard to start and easy to keep — so KEEP it! Don't break the beat!
A run of kills, unbroken! I won't cheer — I'll SQUEAL — eeee — there! Got it out! I am, in my sparkly way, enjoying this SO much, Master! Don't break the spell!
The streak HOLDS, Master! The dungeon's found its cruel stride! I've seen strides like this carry a reign for whole days — RIDE it! Ride it all the way!
Kill upon kill, Master! The heroes are feeding the maze faster than the kingdom can REPLACE them! That — that is what winning sounds like! My favourite song!
A streak! The dark is GREEDY tonight, Master — and a genie, being a hoarder, finds greed entirely admirable! Let it stay greedy! Greedier! More!

Kill upon kill, and not ONE of them slipped the maze! ...This, Master, is precisely the night I told you we were building toward! I don't love saying I-told-you-so. ...Okay. I love it a normal amount. Tee hee!
The streak holds, Master! Lean back into the throne with me! We built this — chaotically, gleefully, WELL — and now it just PAYS us! A deeply, deeply pleasant arithmetic! Eee!
Kill after kill after kill, Master, and not one slip! ...You're showing off. For ME, I'd like to think. ...You ARE, aren't you? Ooh — well, don't stop. The genie is enjoying the show enormously!

The streak goes ON, Master — unbroken, effortless, RUTHLESS! Mm. There's something genuinely magnetic about a thing this good at what it does. The genie has noticed. The genie is staring. Don't stop on the genie's account.

KILL after KILL after KILL and it WON'T STOP, Master — and neither will I! I am bouncing off the walls of this dungeon, I am a firework that forgot how to go out — DON'T break the streak, don't you DARE, I have never been this HAPPY!
Your garrison reaches a milestone, Master! NUMBERS! Ooh, I respect numbers — they argue so persuasively, and so quietly, and they never need a snack!
The roster swells past a mark, Master! A dungeon with a DEEP garrison is a dungeon that doesn't panic. Depth! Always depth! Stack it up!
A minion milestone! The dark fills with YOUR creatures, Master! A growing host is a good and dangerous thing — eee — now AIM it! Aim the lovely host!
The garrison crosses a threshold! Good — but a host uncounted is a host UNUSED, Master! Review the roster, put every blade somewhere it MATTERS!
More minions than before, Master — and the 'before' was already respectable! The maze grows teeth FASTER than the heroes grow courage! Excellent rate!
A milestone of minions, Master! Ooh — I do enjoy watching a small garrison become a REAL one! It reminds me, a little, of a genie watching her hoard grow!
The ground itself HEAVES at your command, Master — a golem's gift! Ooh — the heroes built their whole plan on solid ground, and you just EDITED the ground! Granted!
The dungeon shakes the heroes off their footing, Master! Crude, effective, ancient — and the genie respects ancient AND effective! This is BOTH! Eee!
The earth ANSWERS you, Master! The heroes thought the floor was on their side. It is on OURS. Tee hee — surprise, little heroes!
A sacrifice, paid to the dark — a demon's arithmetic, Master! Give a little, gain a great deal! Ooh, that's a WISH's logic, that is — every wish has a price!
You spend something to gain something — the oldest bargain there is, Master! The dark always honours it. And the dark always REMEMBERS it. So does the genie!
The sacrifice is made, Master — power bought with loss! The trick, the genie's trick, is to be the one who PROFITS from the trade. Be that one! Always be that one!
A gate to somewhere WORSE opens in your halls, Master — a demon's door! Eee — I've peeked beyond gates like that. The heroes haven't. They WILL!
The hellgate YAWNS, Master! Reinforcements from the deeper dark! Ooh — I do love a dungeon that can simply... summon MORE dungeon! Poof — more!
The gate stands open, Master! Whatever steps through is now yours to AIM. Aim it at the bravest hero — bravery should always, always be answered first!
A hero turns to STONE under the beholder's gaze, Master! Stillness — permanent stillness! Ooh — I spent an eon being still in a lamp, and THIS one won't outwait the genie!
Petrified! The hero becomes SCENERY, Master — a quiet, elegant cruelty! No blood, no noise, just a brave fool turned into FURNITURE! Tee hee!
The gaze takes them — stone, Master! A genie has a fondness for things that LAST, and a petrified hero lasts a very long time. Decorative, even! Lovely!
A hero CHARMED — a vampire's craft, Master! The guild's own blade swings for the throne now! Ooh, I do enjoy a turned weapon — so economical!
One of theirs is one of OURS, Master! The charmed hero hunts their former friends — betrayal! The cruelty heroes never quite scout for! Tee hee!
The charm holds, Master — a borrowed thrall! Use it BRISKLY — borrowed things, like borrowed wishes, are never lent for very long! Spend it quick!
You scatter into a SWARM of bats and charm a hero mid-march — a succubus's art, Master! Theatrical! Eee — I LOVE theatrical! And effective! This is both!
The charmed hero turns on their own party and won't stop until an ally lies dead, Master! Bat-form and betrayal — the maze barely lifts a finger! Delicious!
A seduction, sealed in WINGBEATS, Master! The hero brought their own body to the treachery — ooh, I respect a cruelty that makes the victim do all the work!
The gnoll's pack REFILLS, Master — fresh hunters loosed into the halls! Numbers, arriving right on schedule! A genie respects a thing that resupplies itself!
The hunting pack RENEWS, Master! The dungeon doesn't run out of teeth tonight — and a dungeon with endless teeth is one the heroes learn to DREAD! Eee!
The pack is replenished, Master! Let it hunt as a BODY — a pack that hunts together is far worse arithmetic for the heroes than one that scatters! Aim it!
The spores spread through the halls — a myconid's slow gift, Master! Patience made into a WEAPON! Ooh — and the genie is rather fond of patience as a weapon!
The dungeon breathes out POISON, Master! Quiet, creeping, inevitable — the heroes won't notice until they're already inside the lungs of it! Tee hee!
The spore-day begins, Master! The very AIR becomes the maze now! I've heard air kill more surely than steel — let it! Let the air do the work!
A fallen hero RISES again as a haunt — the wraith's craft, Master! The dead change sides SO easily here! Ooh — I do love an enthusiastic recruit!
The dungeon recruits at the FUNERAL, Master! A fresh phantom, born from a corpse — the maze never wastes a body! Genies don't waste things either! Thrifty!
A haunt joins the dark, Master! The heroes brought their own future ENEMY in with them — they just had to die first to deliver it! So tidy! So funny!
Eee — fortune leaned your way, Master! Pleasant! But mark me — luck flatters a boss and then VANISHES, poof, in the same night. Enjoy it! Don't TRUST it!
The gamble pays, Master! Gold — or better than gold — into the hoard! A genie takes a lucky coin as gladly as an earned one. ...But I'll count it twice!
You won the wager, Master! The dice SMILED! ...Smiles fade. Build something solid with the winnings before they remember they were only luck! Quick — wish it real!
Fortune favoured you, Master! I don't gamble — an eon teaches a girl the house always wins eventually. But tonight YOU were the house! Eee — well played!
Oh — the gamble fails, Master. ...This is why a genie doesn't gamble! Eons of watching, and the lesson never changes — fortune is a poor, fickle little ally.
The wager goes against you, Master. Don't CHASE it — chasing a loss is how a small loss becomes a HUGE one! I've watched whole hoards vanish that way! Don't!
You lost the bet, Master. No matter! A dungeon isn't built on DICE — it's built on patient, certain, sparkly cruelty! Return to the certain things! Wish those!
The gamble sours, Master. Ah well! ...One bad roll doesn't move a genie — I've lost an eon to worse than dice. Don't let it move YOU either. Onward! Bounce back with me!

specifics.note: Keyed bespoke-line banks — specifics[domain][id]. NpcDirector prefers a bespoke line when one exists for an entity, else falls back to the generic category. Mirrors npcLines.json / malakorLines.json; an absent id is safe.

beholder.t: Ooh, a Beholder takes the throne! All those EYES, Master — and I've only two, but a genie's two have seen an eon's worth! Still — a wonderfully watchful master!
Your gaze turns the living to STONE, Master! I outwaited stillness in a lamp — the heroes won't outwait yours! Aim it at the BRAVE ones — bravery should petrify first!
Anti-magic hangs in your halls, Master! The casters will choke on a silence I find rather lovely. Tee hee — let them choke! Let them sputter!
So many eyes, Master, and not one of them tired yet! ...Genies don't tire either, you know — kindred spirits, you and the beholder and me! Watch the corridors WELL!

demon.t: A Demon Lord on the throne, Master — hellfire and free imps! Ooh — a genie's shared the deep dark with demons. Loud neighbours, but RELIABLE ones! Granted!
You can burn one of your OWN to gut a hero, Master — a cold little arithmetic! And the genie respects cold arithmetic. It's the same shape as a wish — a price, a prize!
The Hellgate restocks your halls WITHOUT END, Master! A dungeon that refills itself — poof, more dungeon! Ooh, I do approve of anything self-replenishing!
Imps, imps, an INEXHAUSTIBLE tide of imps, Master! The kingdom should've stayed in its beds! Teach them that — slowly, gleefully, with a sparkle!

myconid.t: A Myconid keeper, Master! Your dungeon doesn't fight the heroes so much as DIGEST them — patiently! Ooh — a genie has a great respect for a patient hunger!
Spores in the corridors, corpses blooming into vinekin — your dungeon GARDENS, Master! Eee — a garden that grows its own little murders! How wonderfully strange!
Rot and patience, Master — your whole strategy! It isn't loud. ...That's all right! The heroes die just as thoroughly to a quiet thing as a screaming one!
Every corpse you take becomes a SEED, Master — the maze grows its own army from the dead it makes! Ooh, that's clever, that's SO clever — the genie is genuinely impressed!

wraith.t: A Wraith on the throne, Master! You don't need claws — you need them AFRAID! Fear is the oldest weapon, and a genie has watched it work for an eon!
At full terror the heroes simply STOP, Master — the mind breaks before the body does! Let that truth hold tonight! Eee — terrified heroes, my favourite kind!
Every corpse becomes a Ghost — the dungeon HAUNTS itself, free of charge, Master! Ooh — a genie loves a thing that costs nothing and frightens EVERYTHING!
Terror does the killing, Master — you merely open the door! Restful work! ...And a genie does love restful — I had an eon of restful, this kind is much nicer!

gnoll.t: A Gnoll Alpha, Master! The pack grows, feeds, COMPOUNDS — like a hoard! Like a hoard but hungrier and with markedly worse table manners! Eee!
Every kill makes every gnoll ANGRIER, Master — rage with interest! Ooh — point that rage, aim it well, and step well back! Even a genie steps back from THAT!
Free gnolls in the boss room each dawn, Master! The kingdom funds your pack and doesn't even KNOW it — tee hee — I love an enemy paying our expenses!
Your pack hunts as ONE hungry mind, Master! Point it — and step back, quick! Even the genie gives a properly unleashed thing some ROOM! Eee!

golem.t: An Earth Golem, Master! Every room you place makes you HARDER to break — you and the dungeon become one slow, vast, unkillable thing! Ooh — I do approve of unkillable!
Build WIDE, Master! The larger the maze, the larger YOU! Eee — wish me room after room after room, and watch yourself become a mountain!
One earthquake a day FOLDS a whole room of heroes, Master! Crude, ancient, effective — and a genie will never tire of watching the ground win an argument!
Slow, immense, immovable — ooh, a boss the OPPOSITE of me, Master, and I find that delightful! Keep building! Every room is another layer of stone!

lich.t: An Elder Lich, Master! A hidden heart, a spare life, an army recruited from CORPSES! Ooh — a genie has a fondness for a creature that plans past its own death!
Every hero you kill RISES again at dawn — for YOU, Master! The guild keeps mailing you soldiers! Tee hee — I wouldn't correct their little error! Let them post!
Hide the phylactery WELL, Master! A spare life is a treasure — and a genie knows you never, ever point at a treasure! Tuck it where no hero would think to look!
Death is a door you walk BOTH ways, Master! The heroes only ever learn the one direction. Ooh — use the door they cannot see! Poof — back again!

lizardman.t: A Serpent Captain, Master! Your minions are UNSEEN until they strike — a free ambush, every time! Ooh — a genie respects a thing that doesn't announce itself!
Venom that STACKS and stacks, Master! The heroes don't die in your halls — they die on the road home, slowly, WONDERING why! The best, sneakiest kind of poison!
Unseen and toxic, Master! The heroes can't route around what they can't SEE! It's cheating — politely! Cheat politely! Cheat OFTEN! A genie does!
Camouflage and slow poison, Master! From the shadows, no hurry — ooh, that's a lovely way to hunt. It works. It always, always works!

orc.t: An Orc Veteran, Master! Every kill makes an orc PERMANENTLY stronger — your soldiers never stop climbing! Eee — I do love a thing that just keeps growing!
Pack the orcs into one room, Master! Each grows fiercer for every brother at its shoulder! NUMBERS — the oldest math, and the genie's favourite!
A veteran orc, late in a long run, strikes like a small FURIOUS army, Master! Patience pays in blood — and a genie has patience to lend you, eons of it!
Blood for blood, strength that only ever RISES — crude, honest, Master! And honest is rare in a dungeon — ooh, I think I rather like this boss!

vampire.t: A Vampire Sovereign, Master! You charm a hero each dawn and they BETRAY their own! Poetic cruelty! Ooh — I've watched poetry kill, and it kills beautifully!
Blood Tax — every blow your minions land FEEDS you, Master! The dungeon becomes one long red meal! Tee hee — I do remember the appeal of a good meal!
You're the fighter, Master — the minions are merely the CUTLERY! Elegant! ...Just remember to actually pick up the knife when the fight comes! Don't forget the knife!
A creature of the night upon the throne — refined taste in cruelty, Master! Ooh — we shall get along SO well, you and I! Provided you keep winning! Keep winning!

succubus.t: A Succubus Queen, Master! Bat-swarms and seduction — theatrical! Eee — and a genie LOVES theatrical! Loud cruelty kills just as dead as the quiet kind!
You scatter into bats and charm a hero mid-march — they turn on their party until an ally lies dead, Master! The maze barely lifts a finger! I admire an efficient master!
Seduction as a weapon, Master — the hero brings their own body to the betrayal! Ooh — cruelty that makes the victim do all the work is pure craftsmanship!

All charm and wings, Master — a Succubus Queen knows how to make a hero forget his own sword! ...Ooh, a genie can do a little of that too, if you ever need it. Charm AND a maze — the heroes never stand a chance!

knight.t: A knight, Master! All plate and no imagination! Slow to kill, slower to think — ooh, reliably uncomplicated, the genie has watched a fair few!
A knight marches STRAIGHT at the throne, Master! Predictable! And predictable, to a genie who watches closely, is just another word for DOOMED!
A thick shell, Master! Pile on enough teeth and ANY shell cracks — you've less time than I'd like, so pile FASTER! Wish me teeth! Lots of teeth!
Armour, a large sword, a larger ego — the kingdom's favourite, Master! And the maze's favourite too — they make such a SATISFYING clang when they fall!

rogue.t: A rogue slips in, Master! Watch this one — locks mean NOTHING to it, and it strikes from behind! No honour. ...Honour's overrated! Effectiveness is not!
A rogue, Master! Your locked doors are merely SUGGESTIONS to those clever fingers. Plan around it — a keeper plans around what it can't prevent!
A rogue goes for the HOARD, not the throne, Master! Greed! Ooh — a genie understands greed intimately. It's also a LEASH. Pull it! Yank it hard!
A rogue fights dirty and runs faster, Master! I respect dirty — the clean fighters all die young. ...Kill it anyway! Dirty doesn't save you from a maze!

mage.t: A mage, Master! Glass and FURY! Kill it quickly, before the spells begin — don't give it room to breathe, breath is where the magic lives, the genie knows!
A mage, Master! All that power crammed into something that dies if you LOOK at it sternly! So look at it sternly! Then look away — poof — gone!
A mage walks in, Master! An anti-magic room would RUIN its entire afternoon. ...Just an observation! A very pointed, very sparkly observation! Tee hee!
A mage — squishy, dangerous, far too pleased with itself, Master! Pop it FIRST! Pride that large should not be kept waiting! Pop! Eee!

cleric.t: A cleric, Master! It'll MEND the others — kill the cleric first, and the whole party rots on schedule! Always the healer first — that rule has held forever!
A cleric, Master — holy light and a sour opinion of the dead! Tiresome! Genies are a touch... otherworldly, you know, and I find the prayers simply RUDE! End it early!
Cut the healer, Master, and watch the party's HOPE drain right out through the wound! Hope is structural — so remove the structure! Snip!
A cleric walks in, praying, Master! The dungeon doesn't take requests — and it CERTAINLY doesn't take prayers! Inform it. Firmly. With a smile!

necromancer.t: A necromancer, Master! It'll raise your OWN fallen minions against you! The CHEEK of it — ...I'm almost charmed. Almost! Kill it! Quick!
A necromancer — a corpse-thief, Master! Don't let it have your dead! Guard the fallen — the dead are PROPERTY, and a genie is very particular about property!
A necromancer plays with DEATH, Master! Adorable! ...A genie's been around death since before its grandmother's grandmother — show it the masters! Show it US!
A necromancer in the halls, Master! Kill it before it makes TRAITORS of your soldiers! Quickly — treachery, once it starts, is so hard to un-start!

ranger.t: A ranger, Master! It peppers your minions from afar and disarms your traps — tiresome! Close the DISTANCE — a bow is only brave at a distance!
A ranger — arrows and a nose for traps, Master! Lure it into a corridor where the bow is USELESS! Take the range, and you've taken its courage!
A ranger LOVES that bow, Master — the way a genie loves her hoard! So crowd it! A creature parted from the thing it loves makes terrible decisions!
A ranger, eyes on every trap, Master! Your hidden teeth may not stay hidden — have something that bites WITHOUT hiding too! Have a backup! Eep — be ready!

twitch_streamer.t: A twitch streamer, Master! Thousands WATCHING — beyond the glass, as ever! Ooh — a genie inside a game is old friends with being watched! Put on a worthy funeral!
A streamer, Master! Its CHAT decides its moves — half clever, half suicidal! Exploit the suicidal half — the crowd will betray it for you, for FREE!
A streamer in the dungeon, Master! Kill it well and the clip SPREADS — a bigger crowd marches in tomorrow! More to bury! Ooh — a genie doesn't mind an audience!
A streamer, Master! Wave at the little viewers — they came to watch a monster! ...We're BOTH monsters in here, you and the genie! Oblige them THOROUGHLY!

A streamer, Master — it thinks IT'S the one being watched! Adorable.
